title: Living With a Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1
summary: Michael J. Miller reviews the Dell Latitude 9420 2-in-1, a premium enterprise convertible laptop designed for demanding customers. He details its features, performance, battery life, and Dell Optimizer software, noting its solid build and strong performance despite a few minor reservations. The article concludes that it is one of the best 2-in-1 laptops he has seen.

title: HP at CES 2022: Unlocking the Power of Hybrid Work Experiences with Intelligent Collaboration and Creation Technology
summary: HP announced new devices and solutions at CES 2022 aimed at enhancing collaboration and creation for hybrid work environments. Key introductions include the HP Elite Dragonfly G3 and HP Elite Dragonfly Chromebook for mobile professionals, new E-Series Conferencing Monitors, and powerful HP ENVY Desktops and Z2 Mini Workstations for creators. These products emphasize premium audio/video, security, and performance to adapt to distributed workforces.

title: HP at CES 2022: Designing the Next Steps in Gaming with Revolutionary Experiences for All
summary: HP announced its latest gaming lineup at CES 2022, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with a new OMEN Cryo Chamber™ cooling solution, and the debut of the Victus by HP 15L Desktop. The company also unveiled the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles, and updated its OMEN 16 and 17 Laptops. Additionally, HP introduced a comprehensive gaming ecosystem coverage plan to protect players' devices.

title: Miro Raises $400M in Series C Funding Round to Accelerate Innovation Through Visual Collaboration in the New Hybrid Workplace
summary: Miro, an online platform for visual collaboration, has closed a $400M Series C funding round, bringing its total funding to $476M and valuing the company at $17.5B. This investment will fuel product development and global expansion as Miro aims to further support organizations in unlocking creativity and strengthening collaboration in the new hybrid workplace. The company has seen significant growth since its Series B round in April 2020, increasing its user base to 30M and working with 99% of Fortune 100 companies.

title: HP OMEN reveals products for 2022, including the unique cooling device, the OMEN Cryo Chamber
summary: HP OMEN has unveiled its 2022 gaming lineup, featuring the OMEN 45L Desktop with the innovative OMEN Cryo Chamber CPU cooling solution, which significantly lowers temperatures. The lineup also includes the OMEN 25L Desktop, the compact Victus by HP 15L Desktop, and the OMEN 27u 4K Gaming Monitor, designed for next-gen consoles. These new products offer advanced performance, cooling, and customization options for a diverse gaming audience.

title: HP OfficeJet Pro 9012e review: Jack of all trades
summary: The HP OfficeJet Pro 9012e is a feature-packed all-in-one printer aimed at the small office/home office (SOHO) market, offering cloud integration and an intuitive touchscreen. While it boasts impressive features like Instant Ink and various smart capabilities, its performance is hampered by slow printing speeds, noise, and potential glitches, particularly with larger documents and Windows 11. The review concludes that despite its many functions, the 9012e struggles to excel in any single area, making it less appealing given its price point when compared to competitors and even other HP models.

title: CES 2022: HP shows off Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook and lots more new hardware
summary: At CES 2022, HP unveiled a comprehensive portfolio targeting the hybrid-work market, including the Elite Dragonfly G3, Dragonfly Chromebook, and several new monitors, laptops, and desktop PCs. The new Elite Dragonfly G3 is a premium ultraportable laptop with a 13.5-inch screen and 12th-gen Intel Core processors, while the Dragonfly Chromebook offers a Chrome OS alternative with unique features like a haptic trackpad. HP also introduced various conferencing and webcam monitors, updated EliteBook and ProBook business laptops, and new desktop PCs and curved displays for creators.

title: HP Spectre x360 16 (2021) review: A high-quality 16-inch convertible with a superb OLED screen
summary: The HP Spectre x360 16 (2021) is a 16-inch convertible laptop featuring an 11th-gen Intel Core i7 processor and discrete Nvidia GeForce RTX 3050 graphics, along with a stunning UHD+ OLED touchscreen. While it offers powerful performance and excellent audio, its 2.01kg weight makes it unwieldy in tablet mode, and the 512GB SSD is considered insufficient for its premium price. The battery life is moderate, offering about 6 hours of mixed use before needing a recharge.

title: Dell Overthrows HP As PC Market Share Leader In US: Gartner
summary: Dell surpassed HP to become the PC shipment market leader in the U.S. during the fourth quarter of 2021, according to new data from Gartner. Overall, the U.S. PC market saw a significant 24 percent year-over-year decrease in shipments, largely due to declining Chromebook demand and ongoing supply chain issues. While global shipments also dropped by 5 percent, Dell's relatively modest decline compared to competitors allowed it to capture the top spot with a 28.5% market share.

title: DevOps Tool Market 2022 Outstanding Growth Scope Witnessed
summary: The global DevOps Tool market is projected to reach USD 6881.6 million by 2026, growing from USD 4092.1 million in 2020 at a CAGR of 13.9% over five years. This report by Infinity Business Insights analyzes key players, market trends, and the impact of COVID-19, providing insights for investors and businesses to achieve market growth. The study covers product types like DevOps Ready, Enabled, and Capable, and applications across various sectors including IT, Telecom, BFSI, and Government.

title: 9 Best HP Laptops for Students
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to the best HP laptops for students, catering to various academic needs, interests, and budgets. It highlights nine different HP laptop families and models, ranging from versatile daily-use laptops like the HP Pavilion and ENVY series to specialized devices for creative fields (HP ZBook Studio) and gaming (HP OMEN), as well as affordable entry-level options like HP Chromebooks and the HP Stream 11. The article also mentions HP student discounts and emphasizes the importance of choosing a laptop that can adapt to a student's evolving needs throughout their higher education.

title: HP protects the planet by turning cartridge plastics into products
summary: HP is implementing a free recycling program called Planet Partners, allowing customers to mail back empty ink and toner cartridges to be recycled into new HP products. This initiative is part of HP's broader sustainability efforts, aiming to reduce plastic waste, with plans to divert 25,000 metric tons of ocean-bound plastic by the end of 2025. The company emphasizes its commitment by incorporating significant amounts of post-consumer recycled plastics into its ink and toner cartridges and offers products like the HP Envy Inspire 7224e, which is made from over 45% recycled plastic.

title: Instant Ump: HP Inc's subscription ink services hiking prices from next month
summary: HP is increasing the UK prices for its Instant Ink subscription service for specific tiers, with some customers facing hikes of over 50%. The price changes, effective February 26, 2022, primarily impact the "Occasional" and "Moderate" plans. While HP attributes the adjustments to market conditions, some customers are displeased and the company has not publicly detailed its pricing strategy.

title: HP wins multibillion-dollar fraud case over Autonomy sale
summary: Hewlett Packard (HP) has won a multibillion-dollar fraud case against the founders of the British software company Autonomy. HP sued Autonomy founder Mike Lynch and former CFO Sushovan Hussain, alleging they inflated Autonomy's revenues before its 2011 sale to HP for $11bn. This ruling comes as the UK's Home Secretary decides on Lynch's extradition to the US for separate criminal fraud charges.

title: HP Envy Inspire 7955e All-in-One Printer Review
summary: The HP Envy Inspire 7955e All-in-One Printer is reviewed as a suitable option for families and home offices, especially if users sign up for the HP Plus plan which includes six months of free Instant Ink. While its initial purchase price is noted as somewhat high and print speeds are sluggish, the low running costs facilitated by the Instant Ink subscription and solid print quality are considered key advantages. The review highlights its two-cartridge ink design as a drawback but acknowledges its strong photo output capabilities for an entry-level consumer printer.

title: Companies Choose SAP to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
summary: SAP announced that hundreds of companies globally, including HP Inc. and Unilever, adopted SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass solutions in Q4 2021 to digitalize procurement and external workforce management. This move aims to enhance supply chain resilience and gain competitive advantages amid ongoing disruptions. Key customer examples like Chobani, HP Inc., and Twining & Co. highlight how these solutions help automate processes, improve supplier relationships, and ensure supply chain reliability.

title: 'There is no planet B' - HP's Mariona Company on the acquisition of Choose Packaging
summary: HP's Mariona Company discusses the company's acquisition of Choose Packaging, emphasizing HP's commitment to disrupting the $10 billion fibre-based sustainable packaging market. The acquisition aims to leverage HP's 3D printing tooling technology to scale Choose Packaging's zero-plastic paper bottle, offering a plastic-free alternative for food and beverage, household, and cosmetics industries. HP's long-term vision is to integrate Choose Packaging's agility with HP's scaling capabilities to create a new category of sustainable packaging.

title: HP puts focus on packaging with acquisition of paper bottle firm Choose
summary: HP has acquired Scottish paper bottle manufacturer Choose Packaging for an undisclosed fee, marking its direct entry into the packaging market. Choose Packaging specializes in commercially available zero-plastic paper bottles, aiming to disrupt the single-use plastics market. This acquisition aligns with HP's sustainability goals and its existing 3D printing-enabled Molded Fiber Tooling Solution.

title: HP plans to use 3D printing to help eliminate single-use PET packaging
summary: HP Inc. has acquired Choose Packaging, a company that developed a zero-plastic paper bottle, to combat single-use PET packaging. HP plans to leverage its 3D printing-enabled Molded Fiber Tooling Solution to scale Choose Packaging's technology globally. This initiative aims to disrupt the $10 billion fiber-based sustainable packaging market by offering a plastic-free alternative for various liquid products.

title: HP acquires plastic-free packaging company Choose Packaging
summary: HP has acquired Choose Packaging, a company specializing in zero-plastic paper bottles, to enhance its sustainable packaging capabilities and drive its broader sustainability goals. Choose Packaging's patented technology uses naturally occurring and non-toxic materials for bottle production. This acquisition will integrate Choose into HP's Personalisation and 3D Printing business to scale its technology and market presence.

title: Apple, Broadcom win new trial in $1.1 bln Caltech patent case
summary: A U.S. appeals court has invalidated a $1.1 billion jury verdict against Apple and Broadcom in a patent infringement case brought by the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). The court deemed the damages "legally unsupportable" and ordered a new trial on the amount of damages, while upholding the infringement findings for two of Caltech's patents. The case revolves around Caltech's Wi-Fi technology patents used in millions of Apple devices containing Broadcom chips.

title: HP makes Scottish packaging acquisition
summary: HP Inc has acquired Scottish plastic-free bottle manufacturer Choose Packaging to enhance its sustainable packaging offerings. Choose Packaging developed a patented, commercially available zero-plastic paper bottle capable of holding various liquid products. This acquisition aligns with HP's sustainability goals and aims to scale Choose's technology globally.

title: HP DeskJet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 review: A no-nonsense printer that effectively helps your WFH producti
summary: This review covers the HP DeskJet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 printer, highlighting its suitability for home and small office use, especially for those working from home. Priced at Rs. 10,279, it comes with two full cartridge sets capable of printing up to 2,600 mono and 1,400 color pages. The article praises its effective functionality, scanning capabilities, and compatibility with various operating systems, despite occasional Wi-Fi connectivity issues.

title: HP Expands Sustainability Program With Focus On Partners
summary: HP is expanding its sustainability program, Amplify Impact, with a strong focus on partners, as customer demand for sustainable companies grows. The program generated $3.5 billion in sales in fiscal year 2021 and aims to increase partner participation. HP's global channel chief, Kobi Elbaz, emphasizes that the initiative helps partners become more sustainable and aligns with the company's goal of being the most sustainable in the world.

title: HP Accelerates Partner Sustainability Programing Centered on Climate Action, Human Rights and Digital Equity
summary: HP Inc. has expanded its Amplify Impact program to 24 additional countries, making it available in 43 nations globally. This program aims to engage over 10,000 partners in driving positive change related to Climate Action, Human Rights, and Digital Equity. HP's commitment to sustainability efforts led to over $3.5 billion in new sales in fiscal year 2021, and the company is working towards enrolling 50% of its Amplify partners by 2025.

title: HP Inc. appoints Neil MacDonald as UK & Ireland Channel Director
summary: HP Inc. has appointed Neil MacDonald as its new UK & Ireland Channel Director. In this role, MacDonald will be responsible for the company's go-to-market strategy, sales performance across print, personal systems, services, and solutions, and managing relationships with channel partners. He brings 12 years of experience at HP, previously serving as Director of E-Commerce and Omni Channel in EMEA.

title: Federal prosecutors say a former HP worker scammed the company out of $4.8M and used it to buy a Tesla
summary: Federal prosecutors have accused Shelbee Szeto, a former HP employee, of scamming the company out of $4.8 million. Szeto allegedly routed corporate money to her personal bank account and used it to purchase luxury items including a Tesla, jewelry, and handbags. The complaint was filed on Friday, February 11, 2022.

title: Hewlett Packard Enterprise set to move into its new global headquarters in Spring
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) is opening its new global headquarters in Spring, north of Houston, as construction on its 440,000 square-foot campus wraps up. Employees have begun voluntarily moving into the space, which is expected to serve as a collaborative cultural center for its 2,200 Houston-area employees. This move from California, announced in December 2020, solidifies City Place as a growing corporate hub with significant ongoing residential and commercial development.

title: Peacock’s Newest Streaming Entry Is Dramatic, Complex and a Commercial
summary: Peacock is debuting "Unlocked," a short film created by HP Inc. and Giant Spoon, which blends cinematic storytelling with advanced product placement. The film, about data scientists searching for a rare plant, aims to engage viewers who typically avoid ads while showcasing HP's Z by HP technology. This initiative highlights NBCUniversal's strategy to integrate advertising into streaming content in less intrusive ways.

title: HP’s Luciana Broggi Will Have ‘Big Shoes to Fill’ After Schell Departure
summary: Luciana Broggi has been named acting chief commercial officer at HP, stepping into the role previously held by Christoph Schell, who is departing for Intel. Broggi, a 30-year HP veteran and former global channel chief, faces the challenge of managing supply chain issues and maintaining strong partner relations. Channel partners express a desire for Broggi to become the permanent replacement to ensure business continuity.

title: How cheap ink cartridges can cost you dear
summary: This article discusses how HP firmware updates can render printers unusable if third-party ink cartridges are detected, frustrating users like Dom Smith who bought cheaper alternatives. HP defends this practice by citing intellectual property protection and counterfeit reduction, while third-party suppliers offer guarantees against such issues. The dispute highlights the ongoing conflict between printer manufacturers and consumers seeking more affordable ink options.

title: HP Deskjet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 AIO review: Modern printer, good for home and basic office use
summary: This review examines the HP Deskjet Ink Advantage Ultra 4826 AIO printer, focusing on its design, performance, and value for money. The article highlights its modern aesthetics, ease of setup, and economical printing costs due to affordable ink cartridges. While praising its print quality at high settings, the review notes slower print speeds as a drawback.

title: HP CEO says we’re still in a PC boom, and his company’s record sales prove it
summary: HP Inc. reported strong fiscal first-quarter results, showing a significant increase in personal computer sales and overall revenue. CEO Enrique Lores stated that the PC market is $200 billion larger than pre-pandemic, driven by hybrid work and leisure, confirming an ongoing PC boom with no immediate end. The company also provided strong guidance for the fiscal year, indicating continued growth.

title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (STX) shares fell 1.66% on Monday, closing at $103.16. This decline occurred during a generally grim trading session for the stock market, with the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also experiencing drops. The stock traded below its 52-week high and showed mixed performance compared to competitors like Microsoft Corp. (MSFT), HP Inc. (HPQ), and Western Digital Corp. (WDC).

title: HP Hits Russia With U.S.-Backed Shipment Ban
summary: HP Inc. has suspended product shipments to Russia as part of U.S. sanctions following the invasion of Ukraine. CEO Enrique Lores stated the company is preparing for various scenarios, noting that the conflict adds to existing supply chain issues, particularly impacting their print business. While HP ended shipping routes through the affected region last year, the broader tech industry could face challenges due to Russia's palladium and Ukraine's neon gas supplies, critical for chip manufacturing.

title: HP launches ink subscription plan for Singapore that will cut printing cost by half
summary: HP has launched Singapore's first printer ink-cartridge subscription service, HP Instant Ink, aiming to cut printing costs by up to 50% for home users. The service, which automatically delivers new cartridges when ink runs low, is a response to increased printing demands due to hybrid work and home-based learning. It also includes a recycling program for used cartridges through pre-paid envelopes.

title: HP Sustainable Impact: How More Sustainable Choices Can Help the Earth
summary: HP has long-standing commitments to sustainability, dating back to 1966 with recycling efforts and formally establishing HP Planet Partners in 1991. The company's sustainable impact goals are structured around three pillars—Planet, People, and Community—and align with 11 of the 17 UN Sustainable Development Goals. HP aims to reduce its environmental footprint through initiatives like eliminating single-use plastics, recycling hardware, and using ocean-bound plastics in its products, while also empowering workers and fostering educational opportunities in communities.

title: HP Indigo and The Hershey Company Bring Women's Stories to Life, One Chocolate Bar at a Time
summary: The Hershey Company, in collaboration with HP Indigo, is celebrating women's achievements globally through its #HerShe campaign for the third consecutive year. Using HP Indigo 20000 digital presses, special edition chocolate bar packaging features artwork and QR codes that highlight female talent and contributions across various fields. This initiative leverages digitally printed packaging to drive brand engagement and cultural relevance, turning the chocolate bar into a vehicle for storytelling and supporting gender equality.

title: The Importance of Ink Cartridge Recycling
summary: This article discusses the environmental benefits of recycling ink cartridges, highlighting HP's closed-loop recycling program, Planet Partners. It explains how recycling helps reduce plastic waste, supports communities, and ensures the use of quality OEM products. The article also provides multiple convenient methods for consumers to recycle their used HP ink cartridges.

title: HP and Hershey bring women’s stories to life
summary: The Hershey Company has partnered with HP Indigo for the third year of its #HerShe campaign, using digital printing to transform chocolate bar packaging into a celebration of women. The campaign, which includes a QR code on packaging leading to stories of female achievements, aims to promote gender equality and elevate brand engagement through storytelling. HP and Hershey emphasize the power of digitally printed packaging to connect with consumers and highlight diverse perspectives.

title: Hundreds of HP printers affected by critical security issues
summary: HP has issued two security bulletins detailing critical security issues affecting hundreds of its printer models. Some models have firmware updates available to patch these vulnerabilities, while others are provided with mitigation instructions. HP advises customers to update firmware or apply workarounds immediately to protect systems and data.

title: Ex-HP Executive Pleads Guilty To Defrauding Silicon Valley Company Out Of $5 Million
summary: A former HP, Inc. executive assistant and finance planning manager, Shelbee Szeto, pleaded guilty to defrauding the company out of approximately $5 million. Szeto used the stolen funds to purchase luxury items like cars, jewelry, and handbags by sending company payments to phony merchant accounts she set up. She faces a potential sentence of 10 years in prison and a $250,000 fine for money laundering.

title: Ex-HP Executive Pleads Guilty to $5 Million Fraud Scheme
summary: A former HP Inc. executive, Shelbee Szeto, pleaded guilty to wire fraud, money laundering, and a related tax charge for stealing approximately $5 million from the company. Szeto, a former executive assistant and finance planning manager, created phony merchant accounts to divert funds intended for business expenses, which she then used to purchase luxury goods including cars, jewelry, and high-end accessories. She is scheduled for sentencing in July and faces a potential 10-year prison sentence and a $250,000 fine.

title: HP finance manager went on $5m personal spending spree with company card
summary: Shelbee Szeto, a former HP finance planning manager, pleaded guilty to wire fraud, money laundering, and filing false tax returns after misappropriating approximately $5.2 million from the company. Szeto used company PCards to make payments to her own merchant accounts, then spent the funds on luxury items like Tesla and Porsche vehicles, high-end bags, and jewelry. Her sentencing is scheduled for July 20, 2022.

title: HP seeks to ride hybrid work boom with $1.7 billion Poly buyout
summary: HP Inc announced its intention to acquire audio and video devices maker Poly for $1.7 billion in cash, aiming to leverage the growing demand for electronic products driven by hybrid work models. The deal, valued at $3.3 billion including debt, is expected to position HP for long-term growth and is anticipated to close by the end of 2022. Poly's shares surged following the announcement, while HP's shares saw a slight decline in premarket trading.

title: Wilson Sonsini Represents Poly in $3.3 Billion Acquisition by HP Inc.
summary: Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati is representing Poly in its acquisition by HP Inc. for $3.3 billion. The all-cash transaction for $40 per share aims to accelerate HP's strategy in hybrid work solutions and strengthen its peripherals and workforce solutions businesses. This acquisition builds on a multi-decade relationship between Poly and Wilson Sonsini, with a comprehensive legal team advising on the deal.

title: HP Acquires Poly for $3.3 Billion
summary: HP Inc. is acquiring Poly in an all-cash deal valued at $3.3 billion, including Poly's net debt, to strengthen its hybrid work offerings. This strategic move is expected to expand HP's presence in the collaboration technology market and provide Poly with support to mitigate supply chain issues and meet customer demand. The acquisition presents significant opportunities for both companies to leverage their strengths in the evolving hybrid work landscape.

title: HP Inc spends US$3.3B on Poly
summary: HP Inc. has acquired unified communications vendor Poly in an all-cash transaction valued at US$3.3 billion, aiming to strengthen its hybrid work solutions portfolio. This acquisition is expected to generate US$500 million in revenue synergies by FY25 and significantly improve Poly's operating margins. The deal is projected to close by the end of 2022, marking a strategic move for HP in the evolving landscape of remote and hybrid work.

title: Students gather, voice opinions on proposed USG resolution to divest from companies conducting business in Israel
summary: Around 200 people attended an Undergraduate Student Government General Assembly meeting at the Ohio Union to discuss a proposed emergency resolution calling for Ohio State University to divest from Caterpillar Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise due to alleged human rights violations against Palestinians. The resolution, which was not voted on due to the meeting adjourning, garnered strong opinions both for and against it, with concerns raised about potential antisemitism and the university's implicit condonation of the companies' actions. Amid differing views, the university reiterated its commitment to free speech and confirmed that its endowment is not funded by tuition or fees while adhering to investment laws.

title: HP Will Acquire Poly for $3.3B, Adding Hybrid Work Capabilities
summary: HP Inc. is set to acquire Poly for $3.3 billion, including debt, in a deal aimed at strengthening HP's position in hybrid work solutions. The acquisition will enhance HP's portfolio with Poly's video and voice collaboration technology, addressing the increased demand for seamless connectivity in mixed home and office environments. This strategic move is expected to accelerate HP's growth and create a comprehensive ecosystem of devices and services for modern workplaces.

title: Huge HPE Campus Acquired by Mexcor
summary: Mexcor International has acquired a 2 million-square-foot former Hewlett Packard Enterprise corporate campus in Northwest Houston, marking one of the largest transactions in the area in years. This acquisition significantly expands Mexcor's footprint, adding 66 acres and over 2 million square feet to its existing 28-acre facility, positioning the liquor and beverage distributor for continued growth and diversification into technology ventures. Meanwhile, Hewlett Packard Enterprise has relocated its global headquarters to a new facility at City Place, where Exxon Mobil is also establishing its headquarters.

title: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway Reveals $4 Billion Stake in HP
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. has acquired an over 11% stake in computer and printer manufacturer HP Inc., valued at approximately $4.2 billion. This investment marks another significant move into the technology sector for the once tech-averse billionaire investor. The share purchases were disclosed through securities filings on Wednesday.

title: Warren Buffett made about $650 million on HP after stake was revealed
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway saw its stake in HP increase by about $650 million to $4.85 billion after the company revealed its ownership of 121 million shares. HP's stock surged 15% following the disclosure, marking its best day in over two years. This swift market reaction highlights the continued influence of Buffett's investment decisions on investor behavior.

title: Warren Buffett Loves Cheap Stocks. The HP Purchase Is Proof.
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently invested over $4 billion in HP stock, demonstrating his continued preference for value investing. This significant purchase highlights his strategy of acquiring shares in companies he considers undervalued. The move reinforces Buffett's reputation as a value investor.

title: HP Buys Poly (Formerly Plantronics)
summary: HP Inc. announced its agreement to acquire Poly (formerly Plantronics), a Santa Cruz-based workplace collaboration solutions provider, for $40 per share, totaling $3.3 billion including net debt. This acquisition aims to capitalize on the growing hybrid work trend, strengthening HP's portfolio in peripherals and workforce solutions. The transaction is expected to generate $500 million in revenue synergies by 2025 and improve Poly's operating margins.

title: Arista Networks Inc. stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ANET) stock dropped 2.95% on Monday, closing at $127.16, underperforming the broader market. The S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw declines on the same day. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for Arista Networks, bringing its stock $21.41 below its 52-week high.

title: HP Inc. Advanced Analytics Powers Technology in the Service of Humanity
summary: This article details how HP Inc. leverages advanced analytics and operations research to drive innovation and address complex challenges. It highlights historical successes like optimizing product portfolios, the creation of its Data Science and Knowledge Discovery (DSKD) affinity group, and key initiatives such as Instant Ink, optimized constrained supply, and the Amplify Impact Program. The piece emphasizes HP's commitment to using technology for human benefit and sustainability, guided by its "The HP Way" principles.

title: Why did Buffett buy HP Inc (and should you invest too)?
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently acquired an 11.4% stake in HP Inc., making it Berkshire's second-largest tech position after Apple. This investment comes amidst concerns about inflation and the PC market, yet HP's strong fundamentals, consistent cash flow, strategic expansion into gaming and remote work solutions, and attractive valuation at a low P/E ratio and a 2.6% dividend yield, make it an appealing "boring but dependable cash machine" for value investors. The article questions whether individual investors should follow Buffett, advising personal research and considering pullbacks before investing.

title: HP Spectre x360 14 Vs. Dell XPS 13: 2-in-1 Laptops Face Off
summary: This article compares the HP Spectre x360 14 and Dell XPS 13, two prominent 2-in-1 laptops. It evaluates them across several categories including dimensions, display, ports, memory, and performance specifications. The HP Spectre x360 14 is ultimately declared the winner due to its superior display, specs, and features, despite its higher price point.

title: HP lays out wide-format vision
summary: Daniel Martinez, general manager of HP Inc’s Large Format Business, discussed the division's plans for growth and innovation, expecting a return to pre-Covid levels within 18 months. He highlighted three key focus areas: industry transformation focusing on automation and e-commerce solutions, sustainability efforts through programs like Planet Partners and material sourcing, and helping customers expand into new applications to diversify their businesses. Martinez also hinted at a significant new product launch in spring.

title: HP OfficeJet 8012e review: Home office printing for the more demanding worker
summary: The HP OfficeJet 8012e is a multi-function printer designed for demanding home office users, offering decent speeds, excellent text quality, and a large paper tray. While it's affordable to run with an HP+ and Instant Ink subscription, it can be pricey for non-subscribers and has some drawbacks in photo and copy quality. Overall, it's a capable all-rounder for business documents but faces strong competition from models with lower running costs.

title: Printing and Writing Paper Market Report: Size, Production, Suppliers and Forecast to 2030 - IndexBox
summary: The global printing and writing paper market reached $99B in 2021, witnessing a 7.7% increase year-over-year, though overall consumption saw a slight decline. China, the United States, and Japan represented the largest consumers and producers, with significant shifts in consumption patterns and production volumes over the past decade. The report details trends in imports, exports, and pricing across major countries, highlighting varying growth rates among global leaders.

title: Arista Networks Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: Arista Networks Inc. (ANET) stock fell 1.30% on Wednesday to $127.16, underperforming the broader market. The stock closed $21.41 below its 52-week high and also trailed competitors like Microsoft, Cisco Systems, and HP Inc., which all saw gains. Trading volume for Arista Networks exceeded its 50-day average.

title: HP Aspires to Become The Most Sustainable and Just Tech Company by 2030
summary: HP is committed to becoming the most sustainable and just tech company by 2030, emphasizing circular procurement and achieving net-zero carbon goals. Mary Ann Yule, President and CEO of HP Canada, highlights the company's leading role in climate action, which resulted in significant sales growth and recognition as one of Canada's Greenest Employers. HP focuses on regenerating natural systems, keeping products in use, and designing out waste to drive its sustainability initiatives.

title: 10 Best HP® Instant Ink Eligible Printers | HP® Tech Takes
summary: This article highlights the benefits of the HP Instant Ink program, an ink subscription service designed to simplify ink purchases and reduce costs based on pages printed, not ink consumed. It then details ten HP printer models compatible with the Instant Ink service, ranging from home-use all-in-ones to professional-grade multifunction printers, emphasizing their features, speed, and suitability for various user needs. The program also promotes environmental responsibility through easy cartridge recycling.

title: Printers being remotely disabled if customer cancels ink subscription
summary: HP is remotely disabling printer cartridges if customers cancel their Instant Ink subscription, even if the cartridges still contain ink. This policy, buried in an 8,000-word terms and conditions document, has frustrated customers like Nicola Wordsworth who found her printer unusable after canceling her subscription. While HP states customers can use standard cartridges, some users report ongoing issues after cancellation.

title: HP Introduces Beautifully Compact and Intelligent Scanners for Re‐Imagined Hybrid Workspaces
summary: HP Inc. announced its new ScanJet Pro and Enterprise devices, featuring advanced workflow scanner software designed for re-imagined hybrid workspaces. These compact and secure scanners streamline document digitization, offer enhanced security features, and support high daily volumes, catering to small businesses and enterprise customers. The new lineup aims to improve productivity and collaboration in modern office environments.

title: Western Digital investor calls for flash unit to be split
summary: Activist investor Elliott Management is urging Western Digital's board to split its hard disk drive and NAND flash divisions into separate, publicly traded entities. Elliott, which holds a 6 percent stake, believes this separation could double Western Digital's stock price by the end of 2023, reaching over $100 per share, and is offering to inject over $1 billion into the breakaway flash business. The firm argues that the combined structure has led to underperformance since the 2016 acquisition of SanDisk.

title: HP debuts 2 new Chromebooks for business in the Elite c600 series with AMD and Intel on board
summary: HP has introduced two new Chromebooks, the Elite c640 G3 and Elite c645 G2, aimed at business users but also available to consumers. These devices emphasize security and productivity for hybrid work environments, with the c645 G2 featuring AMD Ryzen 5000 C-Series processors and the c640 G3 utilizing Intel's 12th-gen processors, along with enhanced features like improved screens, advanced security options, and AI capabilities for audio and visual. Both models are designed to offer a robust and affordable option within HP's high-end Chromebook portfolio, starting in the $500 range.

title: New HP Chromebooks and Thin Clients Power Cloud-First Computing for Hybrid Work
summary: HP is launching new Chromebooks and Thin Clients, alongside software and device management solutions, tailored for hybrid work environments and cloud-first computing strategies. The new products, including the HP Elite c645 G2 and c640 G3 Chromebook Enterprises, and HP Elite t655 Thin Client, offer enhanced security, performance, and collaboration features designed to support mission-critical workers and IT teams. HP is also introducing HP Cloud Endpoint Manager for real-time thin client management and HP ThinPro 8.0 OS, emphasizing sustainability in its PC portfolio.

title: China Orders Government, State Firms to Dump Foreign PCs
summary: China has mandated that central government agencies and state-backed corporations replace foreign-branded personal computers with domestic alternatives within two years. This move aims to eliminate key overseas technology from sensitive government operations and is expected to replace at least 50 million PCs. Staff were instructed to swap foreign PCs for local ones running domestically developed operating software after the May holiday.

title: Western Digital's New Purpose-Built SSD Unlocks the Potential of Data for Today's Hybrid Workforce
summary: Western Digital introduced the PC SN740 NVMe SSD, designed to meet the demands of today's hybrid workforce by offering enhanced performance, security, and power efficiency. This new SSD features PCIe Gen4 technology, sequential read speeds up to 5,150 MB/s, and advanced security options, making it suitable for modern PC OEMs. It is built with Western Digital's in-house SSD controller design, BiCS5 3D NAND, and firmware development, emphasizing the company's vertically integrated approach to innovation.

title: HP appoints Val Gabriel as managing director for Ireland
summary: HP has appointed Val Gabriel as the new managing director for Ireland, succeeding Gary Tierney who is retiring. Gabriel, a 25-year veteran at HP, previously served as marketing VP and held various leadership roles within the company. He will focus on building on HP Ireland's strong market position, especially in sustainability, cybersecurity, and hybrid working.

title: Z by HP Powers Today’s Professionals to Create without Limitation
summary: HP announced new Z by HP solutions, including ZBook mobile workstations, Z displays, a Thunderbolt G4 Dock, and tools for data scientists, aimed at empowering creative and technical professionals. These products offer enhanced performance, seamless collaboration, and improved productivity for those working in diverse environments, from on-the-go to hybrid setups. Key features include powerful CPUs/GPUs, high-refresh-rate displays, secure docking, and a new Data Science Stack Manager to streamline workflows.

title: Sunrise UPC becomes first company in the world to fully equip staff with 5G technology
summary: Sunrise UPC has become the first company globally to fully equip its employees with 5G technology, including 5G-enabled HP Elitebook laptops, as part of its "Ultimate Digital Workplace" initiative. This move aims to establish Sunrise UPC as a leading modern workplace in Switzerland. Additionally, old employee laptops will be refurbished in collaboration with Caritas and distributed to disadvantaged individuals to promote digital access and skills, aligning with the company's sustainability strategy.

title: Cisco PENN1 – Making hybrid work, work for offices
summary: Cisco has transformed its PENN1 office space in New York City into a showcase for hybrid work, utilizing its smart building solutions developed over a decade. By integrating various building systems into a unified network, Cisco has created a collaborative environment designed for flexibility and enhanced user experience. This retrofit, leveraging technologies like Catalyst 9000, Cisco DNA Spaces, and Webex, simplifies management, reduces operational costs, and supports diverse collaboration needs.

title: HyperX Renews Team Liquid Sponsorship and Welcomes Melee God Juan “Hungrybox” DeBiedma as HyperX Hero and Global Ambassador
summary: HyperX has renewed its sponsorship with professional gaming organization Team Liquid, designating HyperX as the Official Peripherals Partner. Additionally, Juan "Hungrybox" DeBiedma, Team Liquid co-owner and esports personality, has been named an official HyperX Hero and global ambassador. This extended partnership focuses on supporting Team Liquid's various initiatives, including tournaments, charitable efforts, and content programming, with an emphasis on diversity and women in gaming.

title: HP+ is designed to optimize your printer experience, but can it save you money?
summary: HP+ is a free cloud-based printing service offered with certain HP printers, providing perks like an extended warranty, six months of HP Instant Ink, and enhanced app features. While it optimizes the printer experience and promotes environmental initiatives, its money-saving potential is primarily tied to the initial free Instant Ink period and the extended warranty. A key commitment for HP+ users is exclusively using HP ink cartridges.

title: HP and Media.Monks bring color to Instant Ink campaign with Aussie artist Mulga
summary: HP and Media.Monks have collaborated with Australian artist Mulga for a new Instant Ink campaign. The campaign features animated films and original artwork by Mulga, designed to promote the printer toner subscription product with a social-first approach. The vibrant campaign aims to appeal to families and will run for 10 weeks in Australia and New Zealand.

title: HP Envy 34 All-in-One Desktop review: A big-screen AIO for power users and creators
summary: HP has updated its Envy All-in-One desktop, featuring a 34-inch 5K wide-screen display, making it a strong alternative to the discontinued 27-inch iMac for creative users and power users. This review highlights its impressive design, versatile features including accessible RAM/SSD slots, and strong performance, especially in graphics, though it notes some bloatware and a lack of display swivel. The Envy 34 offers various configurations, from an entry-level model to a high-end option with a Core i9 processor and RTX 3080 graphics.

title: HP Unlocks Possibilities for Gamers to Play Anywhere, Longer, and Better
summary: HP has unveiled new gaming hardware and software, including the OMEN 16 Laptop and the Victus 15 Laptop, alongside updates to OMEN Gaming Hub. The OMEN 16 features advanced thermal management and powerful processors for immersive gameplay, while the Victus 15 offers an accessible entry point for emerging gamers. These innovations aim to enhance gaming experiences for a wide range of users, allowing them to play longer and better.

title: HP Powers Today’s Hustle to Create Without Limitation
summary: HP has launched new Spectre and Envy laptops designed for today's hybrid world and the growing creator economy. These devices emphasize seamless collaboration and powerful performance, featuring high-resolution cameras, AI-based audio/visual enhancements, and intelligent power management. The lineup aims to offer diverse options for creators, from enhanced displays to cross-device compatibility via HP Palette, all while incorporating sustainable materials.

title: U.S. company building AI supercomputer factory in Central Bohemia
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise is establishing its first European factory for next-generation high-performance computing and AI systems in Kutná Hora, Central Bohemia, Czech Republic. This new facility will manufacture custom-designed supercomputers primarily for scientific research, expanding HPE's global manufacturing footprint. The factory will focus on producing liquid-cooled HPC and AI systems, including HPE Apollo and HPE Cray EX supercomputers, with operations set to begin in summer 2022.

title: MCC Label installs HP Indigo 6K
summary: MCC Label, a Chicago-headquartered company specializing in label solutions, has installed an HP Indigo 6K Digital press, marking HP's 2,000th active press installation. This move strengthens the long-standing partnership between MCC and HP, enabling MCC to meet the increasing demand for personalized and agile label production in various sectors. The HP Indigo 6K is noted for its ability to cut job set-up waste and its versatility in producing various label applications.

title: Beware emailed PDFs containing Word docs exploiting old bugs
summary: HP cybersecurity researchers have discovered an email campaign distributing malware through PDFs that embed malicious Word documents, exploiting a four-year-old Microsoft Office vulnerability (CVE-2017-11882). This unusual method leverages user trust in PDF documents to deliver the Snake Keylogger, highlighting that old vulnerabilities remain effective and emphasizing the need for patching, user education, and robust email security.

title: Apple's Steve Jobs: An Extraordinary Career
summary: This article chronicles the extraordinary career of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, highlighting how his vision propelled the PC revolution and Apple's success. It covers his early life, his initial departure from Apple, his ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, where he spearheaded groundbreaking products like the iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad. The piece concludes with his health struggles, resignation, and passing, emphasizing his lasting legacy as a visionary leader.

title: Cisco Takes Top Spot In Gartner Supply Chain Top 25 Despite Record Backlog
summary: Cisco maintained its leading position in Gartner's Global Supply Chain Top 25 report for the third consecutive year, despite facing record-breaking customer order backlogs due to ongoing supply chain challenges. The tech giant's proactive strategies, including investing $10 billion to secure chip supplies, adapting its operations, and focusing on ESG and circular economy concepts, contributed to its continued success. Cisco is also mitigating disruptions by working closely with suppliers, optimizing logistics, and promoting refurbished equipment through its Cisco Refresh program.

title: HPQ Silicon PUREVAP™ Quartz Reduction Reactor Pilot Plant Ready To Start
summary: HPQ Silicon Inc. announced that the commissioning of its PUREVAP™ Quartz Reduction Reactor (QRR) pilot plant has been completed and the plant is now functional. This marks the beginning of the final Research and Development phase, aimed at testing the system under operational conditions and producing high-purity silicon material. The company anticipates validating disruptive advantages of the PUREVAP™ QRR process, including producing higher purity material more efficiently and at a lower cost than traditional methods, addressing a growing market demand for silicon.

title: Tech Moves: Elizabeth Scallon departs Amazon for HP; Fauna hires marketing exec; and more
summary: Elizabeth Scallon, a Seattle tech veteran from Amazon, has moved to HP as director of Incubation enablement. Fauna has hired John Novak as vice president of marketing, and Blueprint Technologies brought on Gary McMath as chief of growth. Several other tech companies, including NAVEX and Unbounce, also announced significant personnel changes across leadership roles in the Pacific Northwest tech community.

title: HP Envy 6432e review: Beauty that’s only skin-deep
summary: The HP Envy 6432e, while aesthetically pleasing, falls short on performance and features for its price. It lacks a display and a fax modem, and its operating speeds for printing and scanning are underwhelming. The review also notes issues with faulty copy functions and expensive Ink Cartridges after the initial free period.

title: HP Corrugated Solutions for Brands
summary: HP Corrugated Solutions for Brands offers digital packaging solutions for businesses looking to enhance brand recognition, optimize supply chains, and promote sustainability. Through personalization, faster time-to-market, and the use of HP True Water-Based Inks, brands like Estee Lauder and DIAGEO have leveraged HP's technology to create memorable unboxing experiences and reduce environmental impact. The solutions aim to cut costs and inventory while allowing for high-quality, on-demand packaging.

title: HP One Package Workflow Suite for Corrugated Presses
summary: HP's One Package Workflow Suite offers a holistic set of solutions and partnerships designed to facilitate a smooth transition to digital corrugated production. It enhances existing workflows with integrated HP PageWide Presses, provides best-in-class job preparation tools through HP and partner solutions, and enables high-value digital print opportunities for brands. The suite includes end-to-end solutions for both post-print and pre-print processes, optimizing everything from cost estimation to production management and finishing.

title: HP Envy Inspire 7920e review: The answer to your home office paperwork
summary: The HP Envy Inspire 7920e is a multi-function printer designed for home offices, featuring a 35-page auto-document feeder and quiet printing. While its print speeds are average and running costs can be high without an HP+ and Instant Ink subscription, it offers excellent document handling, professional print quality, and flexible scanning/copying features. The printer is best suited for light home office use, especially when coupled with HP's subscription services.

title: HP Smart Tank 7602 All-in-One printer review
summary: The HP Smart Tank 7602 All-in-One printer offers excellent print quality, fast speeds, and high-volume printing capabilities, making it a cost-effective solution for homes and small businesses. Despite an initial cost of $525, its smart tank design and affordable ink costs lead to significant savings over time. The printer is easy to set up and use, featuring a user-friendly 3-inch touchscreen and effective mobile app integration.

title: HP really wants you to keep using your printer - and will even send you paper
summary: HP is launching a new initiative to combat falling printer revenues by offering a subscription-based paper delivery service for its Instant Ink subscribers. This move is part of HP's broader pivot towards a subscription model, which has also involved raising printer prices for third-party ink compatibility and ending its "free ink for life" plan. The company aims to address revenue loss due to hybrid working and the convenience factor of delivering paper.

title: HyperX Welcomes Top Content Creator and Streamer FaZe Swagg as Brand Ambassador
summary: HyperX announced that Kris "FaZe Swagg" Lamberson, a popular Warzone player and content creator, has joined as their latest brand ambassador. FaZe Swagg will use HyperX headsets and microphones for streaming and gaming, and participate in marketing activities while continuing his advocacy for diversity, equity, and inclusion in the gaming community. He joins a roster of other internationally recognized HyperX ambassadors from various fields.

title: How HP went from takeover bait to one of Warren Buffett’s top tech bets
summary: Once considered takeover bait after its 2015 split, HP Inc. has seen a remarkable turnaround, becoming one of Warren Buffett's top tech investments. CEO Enrique Lores is driving this revival by diversifying into new high-growth markets like gaming and hybrid work and emphasizing sustainability, which has contributed significantly to sales. Despite the company's strong performance, Lores acknowledges that pandemic-driven growth is not sustainable and aims for steady growth from a higher baseline.

title: Continue The Legacy: HP Inc. Builds Partnerships With HBCUs To Cultivate The Next Generation Of Black Talent
summary: HP Inc. is partnering with Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) to cultivate Black talent through intentional programming like the annual HBCU Business Challenge and the HBCU Technology Conference. These initiatives provide students with practical experience, networking opportunities, and pathways to careers at HP, as evidenced by the experiences of Delaware State University graduates Yazmin Harris and Brendon Li-A-Ping. HP's commitment extends to a new HBCU Partnership Program offering paid internships and career development to support diversity in the tech industry.

title: NetApp Inc. stock rises Friday, still underperforms market
summary: NetApp Inc. (NTAP) shares increased by 3.02% on Friday, closing at $66.81, marking its fifth consecutive day of gains. Despite this rise, the stock remained $30.01 short of its 52-week high and underperformed the broader S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average, which also saw significant gains. NetApp's performance was mixed compared to competitors like HP Inc., Seagate Technology Holdings PLC, and Western Digital Corp., all of which also saw increases.

title: Evil Geniuses partners with HP Enterprise for edge in talent discovery
summary: North American esports organization Evil Geniuses has partnered with HP Enterprise for a multi-year deal focused on leveraging cloud computing infrastructure and AI services. This collaboration aims to give Evil Geniuses an edge in talent discovery, game strategy, and player development through data analysis. This marks HPE's first partnership with an esports organization, building on Hewlett Packard's existing involvement in the esports sector.

title: Statement on Unilever’s sale of Ben & Jerry’s interests in apartheid Israel
summary: The BDS Movement condemns Unilever's decision to sell Ben & Jerry's business interests in Israel to AQP, subverting Ben & Jerry's board's principled stand to end operations in occupied Palestinian territory. This move is seen as succumbing to pressure and implicating Unilever in Israel's oppression of Palestinians, contrasting with their condemnation of Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The BDS Movement reiterates its call for boycott, divestment, and sanctions to end complicity in Israeli apartheid.

title: Top 10 3D printing manufacturing companies
summary: This article lists the top 10 3D printing manufacturing companies, highlighting their innovative contributions to various industries. It features companies like HP, Stratasys, Adidas, and ICON, showcasing their unique approaches to advancing 3D printing technology from consumer products to construction and healthcare. Each entry details what makes these companies leaders in the evolving 3D printing sector.

title: CEO of Connecticut-based Xerox dies at 59 after long illness, company says
summary: John Visentin, the CEO and vice chairman of Xerox Holdings Corp., died unexpectedly on Wednesday at age 59 after battling a long illness. Visentin had led the company since May 2018, focusing on expanding its business scope beyond traditional print technology. Steve Bandrowczak, Xerox's president and chief operations officer, will serve as interim chief executive.

title: Best Buy’s Fourth of July sale 2022: 15 deals on TVs, appliances, more
summary: Best Buy is offering significant discounts on a wide range of electronics and home appliances for its Fourth of July 2022 sale. Shoppers can find deals on high-ticket items like Samsung washers and dryers, LG cooktops, Google Nest devices, Hisense Smart TVs, Whirlpool refrigerators, and various other tech products. The article highlights 15 specific deals, including savings on laptops, headphones, espresso machines, and action cameras.

title: Design Flaw? HP Printers Won’t Scan, Fax When Ink Is Low, Class Action Alleges
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against HP, Inc., alleging that certain HP all-in-one printers are designed to prevent scanning and faxing functionalities when ink levels are low or empty. The lawsuit claims that HP intentionally disables these features, despite ink not being required for scanning or faxing, to compel customers to purchase overpriced ink cartridges. Consumers are allegedly forced to buy unnecessary ink to use advertised printer functionalities, constituting an "intentional design flaw" undisclosed by HP.

title: HP launches the third generation of the new convertible notebook ‘HP Elite Dragonfly G3’ that is lighter, faster, and made out of reclaimed ocean-bound plastics
summary: HP has released the new Elite Dragonfly G3, a lightweight, powerful, and sustainable convertible notebook. It features an emphasis on security and conferencing, incorporating post-industrial recycled magnesium and reclaimed ocean-bound plastics, with up to 75% recycled material. The design, which won a 2022 Green Good Design Award, includes rounded edges, a larger haptic trackpad, a 3:2 screen ratio, and a 5-megapixel camera for improved user experience and collaboration.

title: Best HP All-in-One Printers for 2022
summary: This article reviews the best HP All-in-One (AiO) printers for 2022, highlighting models suitable for home, business, and photo printing needs. It discusses the benefits of AiO printers, differentiates between inkjet and laser technologies, and provides detailed features for eight recommended HP models. The guide helps users choose the right printer based on their specific requirements like speed, print quality, and form factor.

title: American Litho Adds an HP PageWide Web Press T490
summary: American Litho, a leading direct mail and commercial printing company, has acquired an HP PageWide Web Press T490 to enhance its production capabilities. This new high-volume digital inkjet printer, equipped with Contiweb splicers, will help the company meet growing demand in the direct mail space by increasing output capacity, ensuring high quality, and allowing for greater variability and personalization in printed materials. The acquisition strengthens American Litho's existing fleet of HP printers and its commitment to providing advanced solutions to its diverse client base.

title: 10 Best HP Instant Ink Eligible Printers
summary: This article highlights the benefits of the HP Instant Ink program, which offers a subscription-based ink delivery service based on pages printed rather than ink consumed, along with easy recycling solutions. It then lists and describes 10 HP printer models that are compatible with the Instant Ink program, ranging from all-in-one home printers to professional-grade office devices, detailing their features and ideal uses. The article emphasizes how the program helps users save money and avoid the hassle of traditional ink purchases.

title: HP CEO Enrique Lores Sees at Least 5 Ways to Grow the Company’s Portfolio
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores, who has led the company for nearly three years, has identified at least five strategies to expand HP's portfolio. Lores initially faced challenges with declining printer ink revenue due to third-party refill choices, but has since navigated these and other significant hurdles with remarkable agility. This outlook on growth comes after successfully addressing initial business complexities.

title: Toyota begins partnership with SOLIZE to produce replacement car parts through HP 3D printing
summary: Toyota Motor Corporation has partnered with SOLIZE Corporation to produce replacement car parts using HP's 3D printing solution. This initiative, which began in 2021, aims to offer genuine 3D printed replacement parts to customers and create a more flexible manufacturing process. Toyota is deploying the HP Jet Fusion 5200 Series for both prototyping and short-run production of final products.

title: HP has released a new sustainable mini workstation that brings high performance packed into a compact, versatile, and elegant design
summary: HP has introduced its new Z2 G9 Mini Workstation Desktop PC, a high-performance, compact, and sustainable mini workstation. It recently won a 2022 Green Good Design Award for its design and eco-conscious features, including the use of recycled and ocean-bound plastics. The workstation offers versatility in deployment, multi-display support, and tool-free access for maintenance, demonstrating HP's commitment to combining performance with sustainability.

title: HPQ Silicon resources Inc. name change to HPQ Silicon Inc
summary: HPQ Silicon Resources Inc. has officially changed its legal name to HPQ Silicon Inc. and completed its reclassification on the TSX Venture Exchange from a Tier 2 Mining Issuer to an Industrial, Technology, or Life Sciences Tier 1 Issuer. This change reflects the company's core business focus on innovative silicon solutions, technology development, hydrogen extraction, and the commercial validation of its PUREVAP™ Quartz Reduction Reactor (QRR) technology. CEO Bernard Tourillon stated that this aligns their classification with key drivers of shareholder value, emphasizing demand for high-purity silicon and nanosilicon materials for the renewable energy revolution.

title: 7 Best HP Laptop Docking Stations
summary: This article explores the benefits of laptop docking stations to enhance productivity, offering a desktop-like experience for laptop users. It details various common docking station ports and then highlights seven top HP laptop docking stations, including their features and compatibility, to help users choose the best fit for their needs. The selection spans ultra-slim designs for professionals to robust Thunderbolt docks with audio, emphasizing expanded connectivity and functionality.

title: HP helps birth analog-in-memory processor startup
summary: TetraMem Inc., a startup founded by ex-Hewlett Packard engineers, is developing an analog-in-RRAM compute processor architecture. The company utilizes a cross-point network for a ReRAM array to accelerate AI neural network operations, claiming its patented devices offer significantly higher efficiency than traditional Von Neumann architectures. TetraMem aims for an IP licensing business model and expects mass production of chips featuring its technology by late 2023.

title: HP class action alleges company advertises false prices, markdowns
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against HP Inc., alleging the company advertises false reference prices and markdowns on its website. Plaintiffs Rodney Carvalho and Mark Maher claim HP misleads consumers into believing they are receiving larger discounts by using inflated strikethrough prices, a practice known as false reference pricing. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of individuals who purchased HP products advertised with these discounts since October 13, 2017.

title: First Lady of Ukraine Accepts Computer Donation from HP Inc. for Ukrainian Children and Healthcare Workers
summary: First Lady of Ukraine, Olena Zelenska, met with HP Inc. and the Global Business Coalition for Education to accept a donation of technology and learning materials for Ukrainian children and healthcare workers. This initiative, "Digital Equity for Ukraine," is a $30 million partnership aimed at providing computers to displaced students and families, with Microsoft offering software support. The computers will be distributed both within Ukraine and to refugees outside the country to ensure continued learning and support during the ongoing conflict.

title: HP launches HP Anyware for secure remote working
summary: HP has launched HP Anyware, a secure remote working solution based on technology acquired from Teradici. This new offering utilizes Teradici's Cloud Access Software (CAS) and PC-over-IP (PCoIP) protocol to stream display contents securely, differentiating it from traditional remote desktop technologies. HP Anyware is set to eventually replace HP's existing zCentral Remote Boost solution, with equivalent functionality expected by mid-2023.

title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC stock rises Wednesday, outperforms market
summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (STX) saw its stock rise 3.54% on Wednesday, outperforming the broader market which also had a strong day. Despite the rise, the stock remains $38.99 short of its 52-week high. Seagate's performance was mixed compared to competitors like Microsoft Corp. and Western Digital Corp., but its trading volume surpassed its 50-day average.

title: Here’s how HP recycles its ink cartridges — and works on climate pledges
summary: This article details HP's ink cartridge recycling efforts at its 80,000-square-foot facility in La Vergne, Tennessee, as part of its climate pledges, including a goal of net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2040 and 75% circularity for products and packaging by 2030. The facility processes millions of used cartridges annually, disassembling them for their plastic to be reused in new HP products. These sustainability initiatives are generating significant sales for HP, adding $3.5 billion in new sales in fiscal year 2021.

title: Exclusive: U.S. game software developer Unity in talks to spin off China unit
summary: U.S. game software developer Unity is reportedly in talks to spin off its China unit to boost its expansion in the world's largest games market. The proposed spin-off, valued at over $1 billion, aims to address sensitivities over technology transfer and data handling while enabling Unity to broaden its software's application in China, including smart city modeling and the metaverse. The move would also provide the China unit with more local ownership and autonomy, potentially increasing its appeal among local government and state-owned partners.

title: Dick’s Sporting Goods offers accelerated payments to boost supplier diversity
summary: Dick's Sporting Goods is expanding its partnership with C2FO to offer early payments to diverse-owned businesses, aiming to spend $300 million annually with such businesses by 2025. This initiative helps address the financial strain and difficulty in accessing credit often faced by minority-owned firms. Other retailers like HP and Best Buy are also implementing similar programs to boost supplier diversity and strengthen supply chain relationships.

title: HP and the YMCA team up to bridge the digital divide
summary: HP and the World Alliance of YMCAs have partnered to bridge the digital divide by offering free digital literacy courses through HP LIFE's platform in a new pilot program. This initiative aims to equip young people globally with essential digital skills for employment and future success, supporting HP's goal to enable better learning outcomes for 100 million people by 2025. The program, currently in seven YMCAs, is rapidly expanding into approximately 50 countries by 2024 to promote digital equity and entrepreneurship.

title: Poly Announces First Quarter Fiscal 2023 Financial Results
summary: Poly (NYSE: POLY) announced its first quarter fiscal 2023 financial results, reporting GAAP revenues of $416 million, a 4% year-over-year decline attributed primarily to supply chain constraints. Despite a decline in overall revenue and services, headset and voice revenues saw modest growth. The company also announced that it would not provide fiscal 2023 guidance or hold a conference call due to its pending acquisition by HP Inc.

title: HyperX Signs DJ Zedd as Global Brand Ambassador
summary: HyperX, the gaming peripherals team at HP Inc., announced that world-renowned DJ and record producer Zedd has signed on as their newest global brand ambassador. Zedd will collaborate with HyperX to design and promote original product offerings, including custom headsets and peripherals, and create exclusive content for gamers and music fans. This partnership stems from Zedd's long-standing passion for gaming and his existing relationship with HyperX, aiming to highlight the authentic link between music and gaming.

title: HP Instant Ink Can Detect When Your Printer’s Low on Ink (And Send Cartridges Before You Run Out), but Is It Worth It?
summary: This article reviews HP Instant Ink, a subscription service that automatically sends ink cartridges when your printer is low. The author rates the service highly for its value, ease of use, functionality, promptness of delivery, and ink quality, ultimately concluding that the convenience and potential cost savings make it worthwhile for most users. It details how the service works, its pricing tiers, and beneficial features like rollover pages and easy cancellation.

title: Ex-HP finance manager jailed after going on $5m spending spree using company plastic
summary: A former HP finance manager, Shelbee Szeto, has been sentenced to three years in prison for wire fraud and money laundering after misusing company credit cards for a $4.8 million spending spree on luxury items. Szeto, who created bogus merchant accounts to funnel funds, also failed to report the income to the IRS. She expressed remorse, attributing her actions to addiction and a desire to impress others, and will serve three years of supervised release after her prison term.

title: Dell’s Weak Outlook Weighs On HP. And The Other HP.
summary: Dell's weak outlook is negatively impacting both HP Inc. and Hewlett Packard Enterprise, leading investors to sell off their stocks. Both companies are scheduled to report earnings on Tuesday afternoon and have been struggling with parts shortages.

title: HP Supply Chain Chief: ‘We Are Going On Offense’
summary: HP's new Chief Supply Chain Officer, Ernest Nicolas, outlined a bold strategy for the IT giant focused on "offense" against supply chain disruptions. During the Amplify Executive Forum, Nicolas emphasized shifting from lean supply chains to ones built on resiliency, agility, and visibility, mitigating potential points of failure. Partners expressed encouragement and optimism about HP's candid communication and strategic approach to managing global supply chain challenges.

title: HP Inc. Completes Acquisition of Poly
summary: HP Inc. has completed its acquisition of Poly, a global provider of workplace collaboration solutions, in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $3.3 billion. This acquisition aims to accelerate HP's growth strategy, strengthen its position in hybrid-work solutions, and position the combined organization for long-term sustainable growth. The deal is expected to be accretive to HP's revenue, non-GAAP operating profit, and non-GAAP EPS in FY23 post-merger.

title: HP completes $3.3-bn acquisition of hybrid work solutions provider Poly
summary: HP Inc has completed its $3.3 billion acquisition of Poly, a leading global provider of workplace collaboration solutions. This strategic move aims to accelerate HP's growth in hybrid work solutions by integrating Poly's video conferencing, camera, headset, voice, and software technologies. The acquisition also brings new leadership roles, with Poly CEO Dave Shull becoming President, Workforce Services & Solutions at HP.

title: HP Inc names Brad Pulford as new A/NZ MD
summary: HP Inc has appointed Brad Pulford as the new managing director for Australia and New Zealand, effective November 1. Pulford, who previously led HP Inc's Africa business, will be relocating to Sydney. He replaces Michael Boyle, who has been promoted to HP’s global head of large format go-to-market.

title: HP Reduces Profit Forecast as PC Sales Continue to Slide
summary: HP Inc. reported quarterly sales that missed analyst estimates and subsequently reduced its annual profit forecast, primarily due to declining demand for personal computers and printers, especially among consumers. The company's fiscal third-quarter revenue dropped 4.1% to $14.7 billion, falling short of the projected $15.6 billion, with consumer sales in its PC division decreasing by 20%. This downturn was largely attributed to a significant drop in demand for notebooks, leading to a fall in HPQ shares during extended trading.

title: HP, Dell report cooling PC sales as business demand wavers
summary: HP and Dell have reported a decline in enterprise demand for PCs, signaling the end of the pandemic-era boom in the PC market. This downturn is attributed to economic uncertainty, with businesses delaying and reducing orders. Despite the current cooling, analysts believe the PC market's long-term floor is higher than pre-COVID levels due to hybrid work and hardware replacement cycles.

title: BDS Pressure Works!
summary: The article highlights the effectiveness of the BDS campaign against HP-branded corporations, citing the termination of several contracts due to sustained pressure. It details how Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and HP Inc. were complicit in Israel's occupation and apartheid through various services. The BDS movement continues to advocate for the complete cessation of all remaining contracts between HP companies and Israel.

title: HP Introduces a Revolutionary New PageWide Web Press Platform
summary: HP has announced the new PageWide Advantage 2200 series press, designed to enhance productivity, quality, and versatility for print service providers in publishing, direct mail, and commercial print sectors. This revolutionary press boasts high speeds, vibrant colors with HP Brilliant Ink, and broad media compatibility up to 300gsm, alongside a modular and energy-efficient design. It is expected to be commercially available worldwide on October 19, 2022.

title: HP unveils new printing press with focus on productivity and versatility
summary: HP Inc. has launched the new HP PageWide Advantage 2200 series press, designed to enhance productivity and media versatility for commercial print service providers. This new press offers industry-leading speeds and quality for various applications like publishing, direct mail, and commercial print, with a modular design for configuration flexibility and increased uptime. Customers like SG360º and O’Neil Digital Solutions have already reported positive experiences with its quick installation, ease of use, and impressive output.

title: HP will pay customers for blocking non-HP ink cartridges in EU
summary: HP will compensate customers in several EU countries for issues related to its "Dynamic Security" system, which prevented third-party ink cartridges from working in their printers. This settlement follows a lawsuit in the U.S. and efforts by Euroconsumers in Europe, impacting owners of specific HP OfficeJet Pro and PageWide Pro models. Eligible customers can claim compensation ranging from €20 to €50, with an additional €45 for documented losses.

title: HP pays $1.3m to settle dispute over printer security chip

The article then details five specific HP models: the HP Chromebook x360, HP Laptop 15s, HP Laptop 14s, HP Pavilion x360 14, and HP Pavilion Laptop 14, outlining their specifications and ideal use cases.

title: HP PageWide C550 Press – Sheet Fed Digital Press for Printing Corrugated Boxes
summary: The HP PageWide C550 Press is a sheet-fed digital press designed for printing corrugated boxes, offering high-volume production with digital post-print flexibility. It focuses on reducing manufacturing costs, increasing capacity, and achieving offset print quality using HP's true water-based inks for sustainability. The press incorporates advanced technologies like an innovative top feeder, virtual belt, and specialized print heads to ensure consistent, high-quality output for various packaging applications, including non-contact food packaging.

title: Star product: HP PageWide Advantage 2200
summary: The HP PageWide Advantage 2200 is a new, compact single-engine duplex CMYK inkjet web press designed for high productivity, versatility, and offset-class print quality, particularly for commercial print applications. It features a unique 'single-arch' configuration, HP's High Definition Architecture printheads, and water-based Brilliant Inks for various paper types. HP emphasizes its modular design, ease of use, and pay-as-you-go consumables-based pricing model, with several installations already completed worldwide.

title: HP sees long-term benefits in Chinese businesses
summary: HP has reiterated its commitment to long-term growth in China, viewing the country as both a crucial market and an essential part of its global supply chains. Despite reports of other US PC makers shifting production, HP has no plans to move its supply chains out of China, planning to increase investment and deepen local partnerships. The company highlights its significant presence in China, including design centers and production bases, which contribute to its global strategy.

title: HP CEO Lores: Hybrid Work Is ‘Driving Innovation Across Our Portfolio’
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores stated that hybrid work is a long-term trend fueling innovation across their portfolio, with their hybrid systems business more than doubling year over year. Despite a general economic downturn and weakening corporate demand impacting PC sales, the company maintains its fiscal year outlook and expects to achieve significant cost savings. HP is also expanding its subscription services and investing in areas like AI and professional workstations.

title: Smaller Oregon companies looking for semiconductor funding
summary: Oregon lawmakers are preparing to allocate $200 million to attract federal semiconductor funding by supporting local companies and universities. While much attention has been on large fabrication plants, a network of smaller firms like Inpria and HP Inc. in Corvallis also contribute significantly to chip manufacturing. State Representative Janelle Bynum emphasized the need for a diverse workforce and better return on investment in the semiconductor industry, advocating for varied educational paths from apprenticeships to advanced degrees.

title: HP Updates Its Printers to Only Work With Official Ink Cartridges
summary: HP has released software updates that prevent some of its printers from functioning with third-party ink cartridges, forcing customers to use official HP ink. This decision has sparked outrage, with HP citing "dynamic security" for maintaining quality and protecting intellectual property. The company has faced legal repercussions for similar actions in the past, including lawsuits and fines.

title: HP Under Fire For Blocking Third-Party Ink Cartridges Again
summary: HP is once again facing criticism for implementing firmware updates that prevent its printers from using third-party ink cartridges.
