This issue, which stems from the company's "Dynamic Security" policy, forces consumers to use HP-branded cartridges by rejecting those with non-HP chips. The practice has drawn ire from regulatory bodies and parallels previous settlements in the EU and USA regarding similar actions by the company.

title: Stacy Wolff—Head of Design and Sustainability at HP Inc.—named this year's laureate of the American Prize for Design for 2023
summary: Stacy Wolff, Head of Design and Sustainability at HP Inc., has been awarded the American Prize for Design for 2023, America's highest design honor. The Chicago Athenaeum and The European Centre selected Wolff for his instrumental role in driving design innovation and sustainable impact at HP, transforming design into a strategic function. His work includes the multi-award-winning HP Spectre, Dragonfly, Elite, Envy, and Pavilion product lines, with a strong focus on merging cutting-edge technology with customer insights and climate responsibility.

title: Poly Studio X Family of Android-Based Video Bars are Now Certified for Google Meet
summary: HP announced that its Poly Studio X family of Android-based video bars are now certified for Google Meet, enabling organizations to leverage Poly's innovative room solutions. This certification guarantees compatibility and provides a seamless native experience with the Google Meet app built-in, simplifying setup and fostering efficient collaboration with advanced audio and video features like Poly DirectorAI and NoiseBlockAI. The Poly Studio X portfolio, including the X30, X50, and X70, offers solutions for various meeting space sizes, ensuring investment protection and continuous growth within the Google ecosystem.

title: HP To Unify Services Into Workforce Central, New Way For Partners To Upsell
summary: HP announced its plan to consolidate over 64 services for commercial PCs, printers, and other devices into a new cloud-based platform named Workforce Central. Scheduled to debut in the summer, this initiative aims to increase services revenue and provide channel partners with new upsell opportunities by allowing them to manage HP services from a single dashboard and integrate them into their operations. The platform will offer a freemium model and tiered pricing, with HP emphasizing that partners will have clear opportunities to generate revenue.

title: HP looks to lead with hybrid work collaboration and productivity portfolio
summary: HP is introducing new products and solutions to enhance hybrid work experiences for companies and employees, addressing challenges like sub-optimal technology and reluctance to return to the office. The expanded HP Presence features across their commercial PCs and collaborations with Poly aim to create seamless collaboration and productivity, emphasizing the importance of technology for thriving in a hybrid environment.

title: 3 Best Desktop Computers for Business from HP India
summary: This article identifies and reviews the three best HP desktop computers for business use: the HP Workstation Z4 G4 Tower, the HP Z1 G9 Tower Desktop PC, and the HP Z2 Mini G9 Workstation Wolf Pro Security Edition. It outlines key features to consider when purchasing a business desktop, including form factor, processor, graphics, memory, storage, and security. The article emphasizes that the ideal business computer depends on specific needs like raw processing power, upgradability, or a compact design.

title: What is Thunderbolt 4? Is it the same as USB-C?
summary: Thunderbolt 4 is the latest iteration of the Thunderbolt interface, offering flexible connectivity and fast data transfer speeds for connecting multiple devices and displaying content in high resolutions. While physically identical to USB-C ports, Thunderbolt 4 has stricter certification requirements, ensuring consistent 40 Gbps bandwidth, support for dual 4K or single 8K monitors, and mandatory 100W power delivery. This makes it a superior choice for professionals and gamers requiring high performance and versatile connectivity, distinguishing it significantly from the less regulated USB-C standard.

title: 3 Best HP Pavilion Laptops For Entertainment | HP® Tech Takes
summary: This article outlines the key features to consider when choosing an entertainment laptop, focusing on display size, audio, storage, memory, and graphics. It then presents three top HP Pavilion laptop models—the Pavilion x360, Pavilion 15, and Pavilion Gaming laptop—highlighting their specifications and suitability for various entertainment needs. The guide aims to help users find a budget-friendly and high-performance device for gaming, streaming, or general multimedia consumption.

title: Cequence Security welcomes HP Pathfinder and Prosperity7 as new investors
summary: Cequence Security Inc., an API security startup, has announced strategic investments from Hewlett-Packard Pathfinder and Prosperity7 Ventures. The company's platform protects web, mobile, and API applications from malicious bot attacks and is designed to secure an estimated $9 trillion in assets among Fortune 500 companies. While the investment amounts were not disclosed, Cequence Security had previously raised over $100 million from various investors.

title: Why you should consider going green with your gadgets this Earth Day and every day
summary: This article highlights the urgent need for sustainable practices in the electronics industry due to the massive increase in packaging waste. HP is presented as a leader in this shift, adopting a "circularity-first" approach to packaging design and material use, leveraging innovations like 3D printing to create more recyclable components and actively recycling used products. The article emphasizes that sustainability is an ongoing partnership between suppliers, producers, and consumers, requiring continuous effort beyond single initiatives.

title: Life and stock lessons from a billionaire value investor Mario Gabelli
summary: Mario J. Gabelli, a billionaire value investor, built his US$1.7 billion net worth from humble beginnings in the Bronx. This article highlights how his early life, marked by hard work and education instilled by his immigrant parents, shaped his successful 55-year career in investing, emphasizing his enterprising spirit from a young age.

title: HP Inc. stock rises Monday, outperforms market
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) stock increased by 0.84% to $29.96 on Monday, outperforming the broader market which saw the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average fall. The rise occurred during a generally grim trading session. The company's stock closed significantly below its 52-week high of $40.79, achieved on June 1st.

title: HyperX Extends Las Vegas Esports Arena Naming Rights
summary: HyperX has renewed its exclusive naming rights agreement for Allied Esports’ HyperX Arena Las Vegas at Luxor Hotel and Casino. This multiyear arrangement ensures HyperX's continued prominent branding and partnership on co-branded experiences. The arena will also be retrofitted with HP Inc. OMEN PCs and monitors, while HyperX continues to supply gaming peripherals.

title: Allied Esports renews naming rights for HyperX Arena in Las Vegas
summary: Allied Esports has renewed its naming rights deal for the HyperX Arena Las Vegas, continuing a partnership that began in 2018. This multi-year agreement includes co-branded experiences and HP Inc. updating the arena's technology with Omen and HyperX products. The arena has hosted over 700 events and welcomed half a million visitors despite COVID lockdowns, contributing to Allied Gaming & Entertainment's (NASDAQ: AGAE) 28% revenue increase in 2022.

title: Steve Jobs: Apple Cofounder and Tech Innovator
summary: Steve Jobs, co-founder of Apple Inc., was an American inventor, designer, and entrepreneur responsible for pioneering revolutionary technologies like the iPhone and iPad. The article details his life from adoption and early education to co-founding Apple, his departure and subsequent ventures with NeXT and Pixar, and his triumphant return to Apple, ultimately shaping modern technology. It also covers his personal life, battle with pancreatic cancer, and lasting legacy after his death in 2011.

title: 6 Cool Printers Built For The Hybrid Work Era
summary: This article highlights six new printers from major brands like HP, Epson, Brother, Xerox, Canon, and Lexmark, designed to meet the evolving needs of hybrid workforces. These printers offer enhanced features in areas such as speed, manageability, security, and document handling. The piece details specific models and their key innovations, including sustainable toner, versatile functionality for home and office use, and advanced security features.

title: New HP and Sonos Devices Accentuate the Ultimate Home Office Workstation
summary: This article reviews the HP E45c G5 DQHD Curved Monitor and Sonos Era 100 Speakers, highlighting how they enhance productivity in a home office setup. The author praises the monitor's ultrawide display and Device Bridge feature for seamless multi-system operation, and the Sonos speakers for their superior sound quality and easy integration. Both devices are presented as valuable investments for improving the efficiency and enjoyment of hybrid work environments.

title: Intel Reveals Fastest Arc Pro Workstation GPUs Yet
summary: Intel has launched its fastest Arc Pro workstation GPUs to date, the A60 for desktops and A60M for laptops, significantly increasing specifications over previous models. These new GPUs are designed for applications like CAD, AI inferencing, and media processing, aiming to challenge the market dominance of Nvidia and AMD. While Intel hopes to attract workstation users with improved performance and quarterly driver updates, some partners note a lack of demand and suggest Intel needs a more comprehensive solution to compete effectively.

title: What Does Logitech CEO’s Abrupt Departure Mean?
summary: Logitech International SA (NASDAQ: LOGI) stock fell 10% after its CEO of 10 years, Bracken Darrel, abruptly resigned on June 13, 2023, to pursue other opportunities. This sudden departure, combined with recent changes in CFO and COO, has raised investor concerns amid Logitech's ongoing financial struggles and a two-year downward stock trajectory. Citi downgraded Logitech's shares to Neutral, citing uncertainty caused by the lack of clear guidance and executive changes.

title: What Does Logitech CEO’s Abrupt Departure Mean?
summary: Logitech's stock fell sharply following the immediate resignation of its CEO, Bracken Darrel, after ten years, raising investor uncertainty, especially since the company's CFO also resigned recently. The abrupt departure amid a turnaround attempt and declining financials, including a 22% revenue plunge, has led to a Citi downgrade to Neutral. The article explores possible reasons for the resignation, noting the lack of a pre-planned successor, and analyzes the stock's bear flag chart pattern, suggesting further downside.

title: Is Instant Ink Worth It? Top Reasons to Subscribe
summary: HP Instant Ink is a subscription service that delivers ink or toner automatically to users before they run out, aiming to reduce costs and increase convenience. The article explores whether the service is worthwhile by highlighting benefits such as never running out of ink, enabling color prints without rationing, simplifying ink shopping, saving money, supporting environmental efforts through recycling, and providing access to HP support and consistent print quality. Various plans are available for both ink and toner, based on monthly page usage, with unused pages rolling over.

title: HP Laptop Buying Guide
summary: This HP laptop buying guide helps consumers navigate the wide array of HP laptops available, offering tailored recommendations for gaming, entry-level use, business, and convertible needs. It highlights specific models like OMEN for gaming, Chromebooks and Stream for budget-friendly basic use, EliteBook and ZBook for business professionals, and Spectre x360 and ENVY x360 for versatile convertible options. The guide encourages users to explore various features to find the perfect laptop for their unique requirements.

title: Class action against HP over all-in-one printers survives motion to dismiss
summary: A federal judge has allowed a class-action lawsuit against HP to proceed, alleging the company's "all-in-one" printers are designed to disable functions like scanning and faxing when ink levels are low, even though these functions do not require ink. The plaintiffs claim this design forces consumers to buy overpriced ink cartridges, increasing HP's profits. The lawsuit highlights a customer complaint and an HP support agent's response indicating intentional design.

title: Judge denies HP's plea to throw out all-in-one printer lockdown lawsuit
summary: A judge denied HP's request to dismiss a class-action lawsuit brought by owners of HP all-in-one printers. The plaintiffs claim HP illegally withheld information by designing its printers to disable scanning and faxing functions when ink levels are low, even though these functions do not require ink. The court found that the plaintiffs adequately argued that HP had a duty to disclose this limitation, allowing the case to proceed.

title: Tammi Morton takes on Chief Security Officer role at UnitedHealth Group
summary: Tammi Morton has been appointed as the new Chief Security Officer at UnitedHealth Group, bringing nearly 30 years of security experience to the role. She will lead the Enterprise Workplace Security Program, focusing on protecting the company's global workforce and physical assets. Morton's extensive background includes leadership positions at Raytheon Technologies, United Technologies Corporation, Hewlett Packard Inc., and Merck.

title: HP INC
summary: HP Inc. announced an expanded partnership with WWF to restore and protect nearly 1 million acres of critical forest landscapes, taking responsibility for the environmental impact of paper used in HP printers, including non-HP brands. This collaboration focuses on forests in eastern Australia, a global biodiversity hotspot, aiming to combat deforestation and restore degraded koala habitats. The initiative positions HP as a leader in corporate environmental responsibility, piloting science-based targets for forests with WWF.

title: HP dips as it cuts free cash flow, profit outlook amid weak rebound
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) shares fell over 5% in extended trading after the company cut its free cash flow and profit outlook for the fiscal year. This revision is attributed to a slower-than-expected recovery in the PC and printing markets.

title: HP Says Recovery In PC, Printer Sales Is Slower Than Expected
summary: HP Inc. CEO Enrique Lores reported that the company's recovery in PC and printer sales is slower than anticipated due to challenging macroeconomic conditions, leading to tempered expectations for the rest of its fiscal year. For the third quarter, HP's net revenue declined by 9.9 percent year-over-year to $13.2 billion, falling short of analyst estimates, though earnings per share met expectations. Despite the slow recovery, Lores highlighted strong operational performance, including improved operating margins and a gain in PC market share, and expressed confidence in HP’s long-term plan, including integrating generative AI into PCs.

title: Molpack, HP Partner to Digitize and Transform Molded Fiber Production in Latin America
summary: Molpack and HP have partnered to digitize and transform molded fiber production in Latin America, aiming to reduce CO2 emissions and replace single-use plastics. Molpack's Guatemala factory has already deployed HP's Molded Fiber Advanced Tooling Solution to produce fruit trays and other products from recycled paper, with plans to expand this offering across its entire product lineup. This collaboration leverages HP's technology for efficiency, sustainability, and the creation of innovative, lighter packaging with enhanced performance.

title: Berkshire Hathaway Sells 5.5 Million Shares of HP. Why More Stock Sales Could Be On the Way.
summary: Berkshire Hathaway recently sold 5.5 million shares of HP Inc., marking its first such sale since acquiring a 12% stake in the company early in 2022. This move suggests potential further divestments from Warren Buffett's conglomerate, which typically sells shares quietly.

title: HP Shares Fall After Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Sells Large Stake
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) shares fell over 2% after Berkshire Hathaway, led by Warren Buffett, disclosed it sold 4.5% of its holdings in the company. This divestment, totaling 5.5 million shares, leaves Berkshire Hathaway with 115.5 million shares, valued at $3.27 billion, representing 11.7% of HP stock. The sale follows HP's recent cut to its full-year profit forecast due to softening demand in the PC and printer markets.

title: HP Expands Latex Range with New Entry-Level 630-Series – Enabling Even the Smallest Print Firms to Harness Best White Ink Experience¹
summary: HP Inc. has launched its new Latex 630 printer series, making white ink technology and more sustainable printing services accessible to Print Service Providers (PSPs) of all sizes. The series includes models with and without white ink functionality, and Print & Cut options, featuring water-based latex inks and carton-based cartridges. HP also introduced PrintOS Design & eCommerce, a web-to-print solution aimed at helping PSPs simplify operations and boost their online business.

title: New HP DesignJet Portfolio to Maximize Architecture, Engineering and Construction Pro Workflows
summary: HP has unveiled a new 36-inch DesignJet portfolio designed to enhance workflows for Architecture, Engineering, and Construction (AEC) professionals, particularly in hybrid work environments. The portfolio includes the HP DesignJet T850/T950, Smart Tank T858/T908 MFP, and XL 3800, focusing on ease of use, sustainability, and security. These new printers aim to boost productivity, facilitate collaboration remotely, and support environmental goals through initiatives like Forest First and increased use of recycled materials.

title: HP Robot Accelerates Construction Industry During Productivity Crisis
summary: HP's first 'State of Construction Productivity' study reveals significant struggles in the construction industry, with a mere 1% annual growth over two decades and widespread challenges in productivity and human resourcing. To address this, HP is deploying its SitePrint robotic solution, which prints complex construction site layouts with high accuracy, boosting productivity by up to ten times compared to manual methods. SitePrint is now available in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland, following successful rollouts in North America and the UK, aiming to revolutionize the industry by digitizing a previously manual task.

title: HP forecasts 2024 profit in line with estimates, boosts annual dividend
summary: HP Inc. announced its fiscal 2024 earnings forecast, largely aligning with analyst estimates, and raised its annual dividend by 5%. This reflects stabilizing demand in the personal computers market and the company's "Future Ready plan" aimed at strengthening its core business and expanding services. HP also projected fiscal 2024 free cash flow between $3.1 billion and $3.6 billion, intending to return about 100% to shareholders.

title: HP Reorganization: Kobi Elbaz To Lead New Global Channel And Sales Group
summary: HP Inc. has reorganized its global channel organization and other groups into a new division, the global channel, sales innovation and operations organization, led by Kobi Elbaz. This move is part of HP's "Future Ready" strategy, aiming to better serve partners and customers, tackle challenges, and accelerate growth in areas like hybrid work, peripherals, and gaming. Elbaz, a 17-year HP veteran, will lead this consolidated group, reporting to Chief Commercial Officer Dave McQuarrie.

title: AI Is About to Remake the PC. Dell, HP Stock Look Like Buys.
summary: The article discusses the impending revival of the PC market, driven by advancements in AI technology. It suggests that AI's influence will extend beyond cloud services, positioning companies like Dell and HP Stock as attractive investment opportunities. The author highlights the potential for significant growth in PC sales due to this AI-driven transformation.

title: HP toner: Purchase from distributor only after B2B registration?Born's Tech and Windows World
summary: HP is implementing a new policy requiring B2B registration to purchase HP toner from distributors in the European Economic Area, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom. This creates a "selective distribution network" where only authorized partners can buy and resell HP products. Customers are now finding they must also register as B2B customers with HP to order consumables, a move perceived as a "classic lock-in" strategy, forcing some to consider compatible toner alternatives.

title: Best Laptops for Doctors and Medical Professionals
summary: This article highlights the importance of reliable and portable laptops for medical professionals, especially with the rise of telehealth. It details key features to consider when choosing a laptop for doctors, such as portability, long battery life, touch screen compatibility, durability, privacy features, and ample storage. The article then recommends five specific HP laptop models that meet these criteria.

title: HP misreads room, awkwardly brags about its “less hated” printers
summary: HP has launched a marketing campaign in Europe with the tagline "Made to be less hated," attempting to appear empathetic to user frustrations with printers. However, this move is seen as tone-deaf given HP's controversial business practices, such as "dynamic security" that bricks third-party ink cartridges and region-locking printers, which have led to numerous lawsuits and customer dissatisfaction. Despite these issues, HP's printing division operating margins have increased, suggesting a focus on locking in customers for recurring revenue rather than genuinely addressing user grievances.

title: Buffett's Berkshire Slashes Losing HP Bet by Nearly 60% in 3 Months
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has significantly reduced its stake in HP by 57% since mid-September, selling off nearly 60% of its holdings and cutting its ownership from over 12% to about 5%. This move comes as HP has been a losing bet for Berkshire, with the firm selling shares below its initial purchase price from early 2022. Berkshire has been actively paring back its stock portfolio recently, accumulating a record cash pile.

title: Clorox Files for Dismissal of Complaint Over 401(k) Forfeitures
summary: Clorox has filed a motion to dismiss a proposed class action lawsuit alleging it improperly used 401(k) forfeitures to reduce company contributions instead of benefiting plan participants. The company argues that its use of forfeitures is consistent with IRS guidance and explicitly outlined in its plan documents. This case is one of several similar lawsuits filed by the same law firm against major corporations.

title: Microchip wins $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant, Oregon’s first, for Gresham expansion
summary: Microchip Technology received a $72 million federal CHIPS Act grant for its $800 million Gresham factory expansion, marking Oregon's first such grant. This funding is part of a larger $162 million subsidy Microchip received, which also includes $90 million for a Colorado plant expansion, aiming to triple the domestic production of semiconductors essential for various industries. The federal grant complements an earlier $11 million award from Oregon and is expected to boost the state's economy and create 300 jobs.

title: AMD Reveals Next-Gen Desktop Processors for Extreme PC Gaming and Creator Performance
summary: AMD announced new desktop processors at CES 2024, expanding its portfolio for gamers, creators, and everyday users. The new AMD Ryzen™ 8000G Series for the AM5 platform introduces personal AI processing to desktop PCs with integrated AI neural processing units (NPUs) and powerful built-in graphics. AMD also extended its AM4 platform with new Ryzen™ 5000 processors, including the gaming-focused Ryzen™ 7 5700X3D with 3D V-Cache™ technology, and highlighted partnerships with OEMs like Lenovo, Razer, Asus, and Acer for new AI PCs featuring AMD Ryzen™ 8040 Series mobile processors.

title: 3D Imaging Market worth $88.4 billion by 2028- Exclusive Report by MarketsandMarkets™
summary: The global 3D Imaging Market is projected to grow from USD 34.3 billion in 2023 to USD 88.4 billion by 2028, at a CAGR of 20.8%, according to a report by MarketsandMarkets™. This expansion is driven by increasing applications in consumer electronics, robotics, autonomous vehicles, augmented reality, and healthcare, with the automotive sector expected to hold the largest market share and the Asia Pacific region showing the highest CAGR. Advancements in technology like LiDAR, AI, and 3D sensing are fueling innovation and widespread adoption across various industries.

title: New OLED TVs, monitors, laptops and prototypes announced at CES 2024
summary: During CES 2024, numerous device manufacturers unveiled new OLED-powered products across TVs, monitors, and laptops. Key announcements included new OLED TV lines from LG and Samsung, a variety of QD-OLED gaming monitors, and laptops featuring AMOLED displays from brands like Dell, MSI, and Razer. Additionally, several companies showcased advanced OLED and MicroLED prototypes, highlighting future display technologies.

title: HP Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) experienced a tough trading day, with its stock shedding 2.42% to close at $29.09 on Tuesday. This underperformance occurred during a broader market downturn, as both the S&P 500 Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average also registered losses. This marked the third consecutive day of losses for HP Inc. shares.

title: Third-party ink cartridges brick HP printers after ‘anti-virus’ update
summary: HP is reportedly pushing firmware updates that brick printers using third-party ink cartridges, a practice HP claims is for security to prevent embedding viruses in cartridges. This action has led to a class-action lawsuit against HP, alleging the company uses firmware updates to create a monopoly in the replacement ink cartridge market and raise prices. Experts, however, largely dismiss the "virus in ink" claim as implausible.

title: HP’s CEO Says if You Don’t Use Their Printer Ink, You’re a ‘Bad Investment’
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores recently commented on a class-action lawsuit concerning HP blocking third-party ink, defending the company's actions by citing intellectual property protection and even baseless security risks like embedded viruses in cartridges. Lores further stated that HP aims to make printing a subscription service, viewing customers who don't use HP's supplies as a "bad investment" due to the company losing money on hardware sales. He presented this strategy as more "sustainable," claiming HP reuses empty cartridges.

title: What is HP Instant Ink (and is it worth it)?
summary: HP Instant Ink is a subscription service that automatically delivers ink or toner to HP printer users before they run out. It offers various plans based on pages printed, not ink usage, potentially saving customers money and the inconvenience of last-minute cartridge runs. The service is customizable, can be canceled anytime, and is compatible with most HP printers manufactured within the last five years.

title: Commercial Printing: Print Industry Trends and Predictions for 2024
summary: The commercial printing industry is undergoing significant shifts, driven by digital transformation and changing consumer demands. Key trends for 2024 include the publishing industry's adoption of short-run and sustainable printing, a strong comeback for direct mail in omnichannel marketing, and commercial printers diversifying with web-to-print offerings. Automation and digitalization are also accelerating, with innovations from HP leading the way in these evolving market opportunities.

title: HP Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) shares notably outperformed major market indices despite experiencing a 1.51% decline to $28.71 on Wednesday. This occurred during an overall negative trading session where the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also saw significant drops. Despite three consecutive days of losses for HP Inc., its percentage drop was less severe than the broader market's decline.

title: Final Assembly of HPQ Fumed Silica Reactor Pilot Plant Started; Commissioning Scheduled for Q2 2024
summary: HPQ Silicon Inc. announced that the final assembly of its Fumed Silica Reactor (FSR) Pilot Plant has begun, with commissioning scheduled for Q2 2024. The 50 tonnes per year (TPY) pilot plant, developed with PyroGenesis Canada Inc., aims to validate the scalability of producing commercial-grade fumed silica directly from quartz, offering significant economic and environmental advantages over conventional methods. Additionally, the company announced an amendment to the exercise price and term of 6,800,000 warrants issued in May 2022.

title: Hewlett-Packard lost $4bn over Autonomy acquisition, London court told
summary: Hewlett-Packard is seeking $4bn in damages from Autonomy's co-founder Mike Lynch, claiming he inflated the company's value before its acquisition. HPE bought Autonomy for $11.1bn in 2011, but the deal quickly unraveled. Lynch, who faces criminal charges in the US, denies any wrongdoing and argues HPE suffered no real loss due to Autonomy's unique technology and HPE's strategic interest.

title: HP Inc. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) stock saw a modest rise of 0.10% to $28.83 on Tuesday, despite a mixed trading session for the broader market. While the S&P 500 Index increased and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell, HP Inc. closed $5.07 below its 52-week high of $33.90, reached on July 12th.

title: Intel’s new Intel vPro platform to power the next generation of AI PCs
summary: Intel has launched its new Intel vPro platform, integrating Intel Arc GPUs and Intel Core 14th Gen CPUs, to accelerate artificial intelligence capabilities on personal computers. This platform aims to introduce the "AI PC" era, offering significant productivity gains and enhanced security features for businesses and educational institutions. Over 100 new device models from various partners will be powered by this technology.

title: HP Reports Sales That Miss Estimates With PC Slump Dragging On
summary: HP Inc. reported quarterly revenue that fell short of analyst estimates, primarily due to a continuing slump in personal computer sales. Revenue declined by 4.4% to $13.2 billion, missing the average projection of $13.6 billion. Despite the revenue miss, profit excluding some items met Wall Street estimates at 81 cents per share, and the company's CEO maintained the annual forecast, anticipating a rebound in the second half of the year.

title: HP wants you to pay up to $36/month to rent a printer that it monitors
summary: HP has launched an "All-In-Plan" subscription service allowing customers to rent printers and receive ink based on a monthly page allotment, with prices up to $35.99/month. The service requires continuous internet connection for monitoring of printing activities and document types, and involves a two-year commitment with significant cancellation fees. Critics argue HP is addressing self-created problems like Dynamic Security with this model, rather than simplifying printing for consumers.

title: HP DeskJet 4155e Review
summary: The HP DeskJet 4155e is reviewed as a compact printer suitable for casual home printing, offering good photo and graphic quality for its low upfront cost. However, it faces criticism for its high operating costs, lack of duplex printing, and reliance on HP's proprietary ink service, making it suitable mainly for users with infrequent printing needs. For those who print often, other options with lower operating costs are recommended.

title: HP Inc. CEO: Security Will Be A ‘Key Advantage’ In Our AI PC Strategy
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores announced that security will be a "key advantage" in the company's AI PC strategy, emphasizing local large language model processing for enhanced security, lower costs, and faster performance compared to cloud solutions. HP plans to leverage its Wolf Security technologies to protect AI models running on these new PCs, which are expected to launch in the second half of 2024. Lores believes AI PCs represent a significant opportunity for channel partners due to device sales and the coming wave of AI-optimized software.

title: NVIDIA and HP Supercharge Data Science and Generative AI on Workstations
summary: NVIDIA and HP Inc. announced the integration of NVIDIA CUDA-X data processing libraries with HP AI workstation solutions to accelerate data preparation for generative AI development. These libraries, including NVIDIA RAPIDS cuDF, can speed up tasks for data scientists using pandas software by up to 110x on NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada Generation GPUs. This collaboration aims to provide a full-stack development solution that significantly enhances data science workflows on HP AI workstations.

title: HP Inc. stock underperforms Friday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) stock saw a modest gain of 0.59% on Friday, closing at $30.74, despite a generally negative trading day for the broader market. This marks the fourth consecutive day of gains for HP Inc., even as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced declines. However, the article headline implies that its performance still underperformed compared to its direct competitors.

title: HP's 6 Biggest Announcements At Amplify Partner Conference 2024
summary: At its Amplify Partner Conference 2024, HP unveiled several major advancements focusing on hybrid work and AI integration. Key announcements included new business laptops and mobile workstations featuring AI capabilities, an expanded partnership with Nvidia for AI Studio software, and a revamped Workplace Experience Platform (WEX) with AI-powered management. HP also introduced new conferencing solutions, an AI MasterClass program for partners, and new Color LaserJet Pro 3000 printers for SMBs.

title: Lilly CEO reaffirms commitment to Chinese market amid US threat against local biotechs
summary: Eli Lilly's CEO, David Ricks, reconfirmed the company's dedication to the Chinese market during a meeting with China’s Commerce Minister Wang Wentao, expressing willingness to increase investment and R&D cooperation despite heightened U.S.-China geopolitical tensions. This commitment comes as Lilly has several drug applications under review in China and amid concerns about a U.S. bill, the BIOSECURE Act, that could impact partners like WuXi AppTec. The move distinguishes Lilly from some U.S. peers, aligning more with European pharmaceutical companies that have recently made significant commitments to China.

title: Streamlining Hybrid Work Experiences with HP and Poly
summary: HP and Poly are enhancing hybrid work experiences by introducing Bluetooth® direct technology for Zoom, allowing Poly Voyager headsets to seamlessly connect to select HP notebooks without a dongle. This innovation simplifies call management for employees and offers IT teams centralized control through the Poly Lens Admin portal. Additionally, HP offers Zoom-certified solutions like the EliteBook 840/845 G11 Notebook PCs, designed to provide secure and optimized collaboration.

title: Building an Inclusive and Sustainable Future with HP Amplify Impact
summary: The article highlights Introstat, a South African IT print vendor, as the global winner of the 2022 HP Amplify Impact Awards, showcasing their commitment to community impact, diversity, equity, and inclusion (DE&I). Through the HP Amplify Impact program, Introstat significantly upskilled its employees, improved DE&I strategies, and worked on carbon footprint reduction, demonstrating the power of partnership for sustainable business development. This collaboration has led to increased revenue, a more empowered staff, and a brighter future for their employees and community.

title: How HP Managed Print Services Can Help Your Business
summary: HP Managed Print Services (MPS) help businesses adapt to evolving work environments by providing scalable, secure, and sustainable printing solutions. These services optimize printing workflows across various industries, from healthcare to retail, ensuring data security, enhancing productivity, and supporting hybrid work models. HP MPS also addresses sustainability goals and offers personalized plans to meet specific business needs.

title: AMD and Nvidia unveil new processors for AI PCs
summary: AMD and Nvidia have both launched new processors designed for AI PCs, aiming to capitalize on the growing market for computers capable of running AI workloads on-device. AMD introduced its Ryzen PRO 8040 and 8000 Series processors, emphasizing their AI-enabled features for business users, while Nvidia unveiled new RTX A400 and A1000 GPUs with enhanced Tensor and RT Cores for accelerating AI and graphics tasks. This move by both companies highlights the intensifying competition in the AI PC sector, which analysts predict will drive new growth in the overall PC market.

title: Lawsuit says HP took advantage of customers’ sunk costs with printer updates prohibiting third-party ink
summary: HP is facing another class-action lawsuit concerning firmware updates that prevent printer owners from using third-party ink cartridges, a practice HP previously settled similar complaints for. Plaintiffs argue that HP is trying to monopolize the aftermarket for replacement ink, while HP contends it never hid the restrictions and that federal law prohibits the plaintiffs from seeking damages.

title: HP & INDO-MIM Forge Path For India’s First Mass-Produced 3D Printed Metal Parts
summary: HP and INDO-MIM have partnered to establish India's first large-scale production of 3D printed metal parts, utilizing HP's Metal Jet S100 printers. This collaboration aims to revolutionize manufacturing in critical sectors like automotive, aerospace, defense, and medical equipment, reducing reliance on imported components and aligning with the 'Make in India' initiative. The partnership also involves innovation in material science, with trials underway for new HP Metal Jet materials.

title: Doyle Family and Gamut Acquire DEX Imaging
summary: Gamut Capital Management and the Doyle family have announced an agreement to re-acquire DEX Imaging from Staples, Inc. Dan Doyle Jr., CEO of DEX, expressed excitement about reclaiming ownership and partnering with Gamut to enhance supplier relationships and service offerings. The acquisition aims to build a national presence through strategic growth and acquisitions, leveraging DEX's strong foundation in managed print services.

title: Endeavor 3D integrates Materialise CO-AM with its fleet of HP AM machines
summary: Endeavor 3D announced its integration of the Materialise CO-AM software platform to enhance its HP Additive Manufacturing machine production capacity. This strategic move aims to streamline workflows, reduce manual touchpoints, and increase efficiency with automation, targeting a 20% increase in labor productivity and a 30% yield improvement. The collaboration with Materialise NV and HP will support growing demand for additively manufactured applications and future-proof Endeavor 3D's operations.

title: Open redirect vulnerabilities exploited in ‘cat-phishing’ attacks, HP warns
summary: HP Inc. has released a new report warning about "cat-phishing" techniques used by cybercriminals, where open redirect vulnerabilities on legitimate websites are exploited to trick victims into visiting malicious sites. The report also highlights other tactics like "Living-off-the-BITS" to use Windows functions for malware downloads and fake invoice lures leading to HTML smuggling attacks. These methods aim to bypass defenses and capitalize on common employee behaviors to deploy malware or gain unauthorized access.

title: HP Unlocks New Era of AI Experiences Powering Work and Creation
summary: HP has unveiled new AI PCs, the HP OmniBook X AI PC and HP EliteBook Ultra AI PC, designed with ARM architecture and Snapdragon X Elite processors to power AI experiences locally. These devices aim to enhance productivity and creativity with features like Copilot+ PC and improved collaboration tools. HP highlights their sleek design, long battery life, sustainability, and robust security for both consumer and commercial users.

title: 6 Big Printer Industry Trends In 2024: HP, Xerox, Epson And Others Weigh In
summary: The printer industry is seeing stabilization after pandemic disruptions, with hybrid work influencing businesses to right-size and decentralize their printer fleets. Key trends in 2024 include a continued push for digitization, growing acceptance of inkjet printers in offices, and an increasing focus on sustainability in purchasing decisions. While some businesses acknowledge printer security, awareness remains a challenge, and customers are seeking stability from vendors amidst industry disruptions.

title: Watch Your Feet! Don’t Fall Into the HP Stock Dividend Trap.
summary: The article warns investors against buying HP (HPQ) stock, despite its enticing dividend yield. It highlights that Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently sold its entire HP stake, suggesting concerns about the company's future. The author also points to the struggling personal computer market, which could make HP's dividend unsustainable.

title: HP Next-Gen AI PCs Plan to Leverage LLMs for Greater Productivity
summary: HP has introduced its new AI PCs, the OmniBook X AI PC and EliteBook Ultra AI PC, designed to leverage large language models (LLMs) and generative AI for enhanced productivity and creativity. These new models are powered by Snapdragon X Elite processors with dedicated NPUs capable of 45 trillion operations per second, enabling on-device AI capabilities. The PCs aim to redefine personal computing by offering features like extended battery life, improved collaboration tools with Poly Camera Pro, and enhanced security with Wolf Pro Security Next Gen Antivirus.

title: HP CEO: AI PCs To Make Up A Tenth Of Shipments In Fiscal Year’s Second Half
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores predicts that AI PCs will constitute 10% of the company's client computer shipments in the latter half of its fiscal year, driven initially by consumer sales. He anticipates a significant increase in AI PC demand over the next few years, forecasting that AI PCs will represent 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years. Despite a decline in printing revenue, HP's Personal Systems business saw growth in commercial PC sales.

title: Rising commercial PC sales help HP beat earnings and revenue targets
summary: HP Inc. surpassed its earnings and revenue targets, driven by a recovery in the global PC market and increasing commercial PC sales. The company's stock rose in after-hours trading as it looks to benefit from the new generation of AI-enabled PCs. HP anticipates AI PCs will constitute 40-50% of total PC shipments within three years, aligning with analyst forecasts for significant growth in the coming years.

title: Pyrogenesis to Exercise Options for 50% Stake in HPQ Silica Polvere Inc.
summary: PyroGenesis Canada Inc. has announced its intent to exercise its option to convert a 10% gross sales royalty into a 50% ownership stake in HPQ Silica Polvere Inc., an HPQ Silicon Inc. subsidiary focusing on Fumed Silica Reactor technology. This move aims to eliminate future financing risks and obstacles in partner discussions for HPQ Polvere, while enhancing PyroGenesis's potential benefit from the FSR project's future success. The Fumed Silica market is projected to grow significantly, offering a strong pathway for the FSR technology's commercial production.

title: 23 Tech Stocks Now Yield Higher Dividends Than The S&P 500
summary: The article highlights a significant shift in the tech sector, with 23 tech stocks now offering higher dividend yields than the S&P 500. It discusses how traditional dividend ETFs are underexposed to technology, prompting investors to seek specialized tech dividend ETFs. The piece also notes that while some high-yielding tech stocks like IBM have seen modest share price growth, others such as Qualcomm offer both strong dividends and price appreciation.

title: PyroGenesis to buy 50% stake in HPQ's fumed silica tech subsidiary
summary: PyroGenesis Canada Inc. (PYR-T) is converting its royalty agreement with HPQ Silicon Inc.'s (HPQ-X) subsidiary, HPQ Silica Polvere Inc., into a 50% ownership stake. This move establishes a joint venture focused on low-carbon fumed silica production, aiming to enhance profitability and minimize financing risks for the Fumed Silica Reactor (FSR) technology. The FSR technology promises significant reductions in carbon emissions and energy consumption compared to traditional methods.

title: HP Acquires CyberCore Technologies; Jennifer Kauffman Quoted
summary: HP has acquired CyberCore Technologies for an undisclosed sum, with CyberCore operating as a wholly-owned subsidiary of HPI Federal. CyberCore, a provider of secure supply chain management and IT platforms for federal and commercial clients, will leverage HP's market reach to enhance tech delivery. Jennifer Kauffman, CyberCore's COO, confirmed the acquisition, expressing excitement about the continued operation as a standalone company and channel partner.

title: Humane, the startup behind the AI Pin, in talks with HP, telecoms to sell
summary: Humane, the AI hardware startup founded by ex-Apple designers, is reportedly seeking a buyer following the lukewarm reception of its AI Pin. The company is in discussions with HP and several telecom companies and has enlisted investment bank Tidal Partners to advise on a potential deal. The AI Pin, intended as a smartphone replacement, faced criticism from reviewers for being untrustworthy and not very useful.

title: Willingness to Adapt Powers HP’s Customer Success Transformation
summary: HP has transformed its global customer success organization to meet evolving customer needs, piloting a new platform for consistent, efficient service across its worldwide operations. This initiative, guided by Vice President Grad Rosenbaum, standardizes processes and empowers customer success managers, emphasizing flexibility, internal communication, and collaboration across departments to support enterprise customers in the hybrid work environment. Despite challenges in adapting a large hardware-centric company, HP's ability to evolve has been key to its long-term success.

title: British tech baron Mike Lynch not guilty of HP-Autonomy fraud in remarkable courtroom redemption
summary: British tech tycoon Mike Lynch was found not guilty of criminal fraud charges in a US federal court, relating to Hewlett Packard's $11 billion acquisition of his software company, Autonomy, in 2011. This verdict represents a significant personal redemption for Lynch, who had previously lost a civil trial in London over similar allegations. The jury also acquitted Autonomy's former vice president for finance, Stephen Keith Chamberlain, after a three-month trial.

title: Silicon Valley life science firm Agilent lays off 184 workers
summary: Agilent Technologies, a Silicon Valley life science firm, is laying off 184 employees across California, including 156 at its Santa Clara headquarters. The layoffs, effective August 9, affect various roles such as salespeople, finance staff, and manufacturing engineers. The company stated the decision was made to adjust to a slower-than-anticipated market recovery and to reinvest in strategic areas, following an 8.4% decrease in fiscal second-quarter revenue.

title: Print Pals Empowers Self-Expression Through the Magic of Print and Puppetry
summary: HP has launched "Print Pals," a new line of printable puppets designed by renowned puppeteer Stacey Gordon to help children, especially neurodivergent ones, express themselves and enhance social engagement. The initiative merges printing with art, offering free customizable puppets at HP.com/PrintPals. HP partnered with Kylie Kelce and The Eagles Autism Foundation to promote the program, aiming to foster digital inclusion and support neurodiverse communities.

title: All4Labels and HP Announce Multi-Unit Press Deal
summary: All4Labels announced a multi-unit press deal with HP at drupa 2024, continuing their 27-year strategic partnership. This investment, which includes HP Indigo V12, HP Indigo 200K, and the enhanced HP PageWide Advantage 2200, aims to fully digitize All4Labels' production floors. The move reinforces All4Labels' position in the packaging converting industry and highlights a shared commitment to innovation, sustainability, and agility, with early unit deployments starting in summer 2024.

title: SK Hynix patents-owned Mimir IP files complaint against Micron
summary: Mimir IP, a patent management company that acquired 1,500 chip-related patents from SK Hynix, has filed a complaint against Micron Technology Inc. for patent infringement. The lawsuit, also targeting Tesla, Dell, HP, and Lenovo, was filed in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas and the US International Trade Commission. This case highlights a growing trend of patent infringement disputes involving non-practicing entities (NPEs) in the competitive chip industry, with potential damages reaching $480 million.

title: HP Industrial Print and ePac Flexible Packaging Enter into Multiyear Strategic Agreement to Convert ePac’s Flexible Packaging Fleet to HP Indigo 200k
summary: HP Industrial Print and ePac Flexible Packaging have announced a multiyear strategic agreement to convert ePac’s fleet to HP’s Indigo 200K digital press. This collaboration aims to enhance ePac's capabilities with improved speed, quality, and versatility, solidifying its position as an industry leader in flexible packaging. The HP Indigo 200K is expected to boost productivity and offer advanced customization and sustainable solutions for ePac's customers.

title: Medtronic announces departure of Karen Parkhill, Chief Financial Officer
summary: Medtronic plc announced the resignation of Karen Parkhill, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, who will be leaving to join HP Inc. Geoff Martha, Medtronic chairman and CEO, thanked Parkhill for her eight years of service. Gary Corona will serve as interim chief financial officer upon Parkhill's departure on August 2, 2024, and Medtronic reaffirmed its financial guidance for Q1 and the full fiscal year 2025.

title: 3D Printing in Education Sector Market size is set to grow by USD 1.10 billion from 2024-2028, Benefits offered by 3D printing in education sector to boost the market growth, Technavio
summary: The 3D printing in education sector market is projected to grow by USD 1.10 billion from 2024 to 2028, driven by the benefits offered by 3D printing in education and continuous product development. Key players like 3D Systems Corp., HP Inc., and Stratasys Ltd. are expanding their offerings. Challenges such as the growing threat from 3D printing rental services and high initial investments are also noted.

title: HP Wins Transfer of USB Patent Lawsuit from Texas to California
summary: HP Inc. successfully moved a patent infringement lawsuit from a Texas federal court to Northern California, where the company is headquartered. The lawsuit, filed by Universal Connectivity Technologies Inc., accuses HP of infringing eight USB connectivity patents with its laptops, desktops, monitors, and docking stations. Judge Robert Pitman ruled in favor of HP, stating the case had no material ties to Austin, Texas.

title: HPQ Silica Polvere Signs Letter of Intent with Evonik Corporation
summary: HPQ Silica Polvere Inc. (HSPI) has signed a Letter of Intent (LOI) with global specialty chemicals company Evonik Corporation, marking a significant step towards commercializing its Fumed Silica Reactor (FSR) process. This agreement validates the commercial potential of FSR technology for low-cost, low-carbon fumed silica manufacturing, with plans for collaboration on pilot-scale validation. Both HPQ Silicon and PyroGenesis Canada emphasize the FSR's potential to revolutionize fumed silica production with improved sustainability and performance.

title: HP Debuts 'World's Highest Performance AI PC'
summary: HP has launched its new "world's highest performance AI PC," the HP OmniBook Ultra, powered by AMD Ryzen AI 300 processor, alongside new OmniStudio desktops and the Z by HP AI Studio for LLM development. The Z by HP AI Studio, integrating with HP’s Galileo AI model, aims to accelerate AI-powered application development while correcting biases and inaccuracies. These innovations reflect HP's strategy to enhance both personal and enterprise product lines with artificial intelligence, helping channel partners gain a competitive edge.

title: HP Expands Financing Options for Consumers Across All Credit Tiers
summary: HP Inc. announced new, comprehensive U.S. consumer financing options for products purchased on HP.com, making technology more accessible to nearly all U.S. consumers. Partnering with ChargeAfter, and financial providers Bread Financial, Concora Credit, and Koalafi, HP now offers various financing options including promotional financing, buy now pay later, and lease-to-own for different credit tiers. This initiative aims to empower consumers facing economic pressures by providing flexible payment solutions for PCs, printers, and peripherals.

title: Qualcomm Launches 5G Processor to Make Gigabit 5G More Affordable
summary: Qualcomm has launched the new Snapdragon 4s Gen 2 mobile platform, aiming to make 5G technology more accessible and affordable in India. This processor offers Gigabit 5G connectivity, power efficiency, and enhanced camera capabilities, targeting entry-level smartphones to accelerate 5G penetration. Qualcomm also showcased its collaboration with PC OEMs to introduce Snapdragon X Series-powered Copilot+ PCs with AI-led experiences in India.

title: Hiscox chairman among missing in yacht sinking
summary: Hiscox chairman Jonathan Bloomer is among six people missing after the luxury yacht Bayesian sank during a storm off Sicily. Also among the missing are UK tech tycoon Mike Lynch and his daughter Hannah, and lawyer Chris Morvollo. Fifteen of the 22 people on board were rescued, but the body of the ship's cook was recovered, and a search operation continues for the remaining missing individuals.

title: Unusual Machines and HP enhance FPV drone production with 3D printing
summary: Aerospace component manufacturer Unusual Machines has partnered with HP to integrate Multi Jet Fusion (MJF) 3D printing into its production of First-Person View (FPV) drones. This collaboration aims to boost the performance and durability of their drones while supporting domestic manufacturing by producing components in the U.S. using resilient TPU materials. The move is expected to control costs and enhance product quality, making FPV drone parts virtually indestructible.

title: What Are the Benefits of Windows 11 for AI?
summary: Windows 11 leverages AI and machine learning to enhance productivity, security, and user experience across various applications. Key benefits include optimized UI with features like Snap Assist and Copilot, improved performance in productivity tools and gaming, and robust cybersecurity measures. HP and other manufacturers are integrating these AI capabilities into their hardware, such as the HP Spectre and ENVY lines, to deliver powerful and secure computing experiences.

title: Biden-Harris Administration Announces Preliminary Terms with HP to Support Development and Commercialization of Cutting-Edge Semiconductor Technologies
summary: The Biden-Harris Administration has announced a preliminary memorandum of terms with HP Inc., proposing up to $50 million in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act. This investment aims to expand and modernize HP's facility in Corvallis, Oregon, focusing on semiconductor technologies that support life sciences instrumentation and AI hardware. The project is expected to create over 250 manufacturing and construction jobs and bolster the U.S. semiconductor ecosystem.

title: HP Computers Made with Ocean-Bound Plastics | Sustainable Tech
summary: HP is actively addressing the global plastic pollution crisis by incorporating ocean-bound plastics into its products through its circular supply chain and HP Planet Partners program. The company defines ocean-bound plastics as uncollected plastic waste within 30 miles of waterways, aiming to stop it from reaching the ocean. HP's initiative also focuses on social impact, creating employment opportunities and community support in areas like Haiti, while showcasing various products made with these recycled materials.

title: HP’s Oregon fab receives CHIPS funding
summary: HP Inc. is set to receive up to $50 million in CHIPS and Science Act funding from the Department of Commerce to expand and modernize its Corvallis, Oregon, facility. This investment will support the manufacturing of silicon devices for drug discovery and medical applications, creating nearly 150 construction jobs and over 100 manufacturing jobs. The project demonstrates a public-private partnership, with HP also seeking the Department of the Treasury’s Investment Tax Credit and having already secured $9.5 million from the state of Oregon.

title: MSA Safety Honored with Outstanding Innovator Award
summary: MSA Safety (NYSE: MSA) has been honored with the 2024 Outstanding Corporate Innovator (OCI) Award by The Product Development and Management Association (PDMA) for the second time. The company was recognized for its commitment to innovation, customer-centric focus, and transformation into a global leader in advanced safety technologies, particularly for integrating Voice-of-Customer feedback into its innovation strategy. MSA Safety will accept the award at the 2024 PDMA Inspire Innovation Conference in St. Louis on September 16, 2024.

title: HP Inc. has a Printer Problem. Why the Market Doesn’t Care.
summary: Despite a 3% decline in its printer business revenue, HP Inc. stock is performing well post-earnings report. Investors seem unfazed by the printer segment's underperformance, suggesting other areas of the business are driving market confidence, possibly unrelated to AI demand in the printer sector.

title: 10 Best Additive Manufacturing Vendors
summary: This article identifies and describes the top 10 additive manufacturing companies globally, crucial for industries like automotive, healthcare, and aerospace. It details the services and specializations of each vendor, ranging from 3D Systems' comprehensive offerings to Materialise NV's full-spectrum solutions, highlighting their role in the rapidly growing additive manufacturing market projected to reach USD 74.21 billion by 2031.

title: Labelexpo Americas 2024: A-Z technology preview
summary: Labelexpo Americas 2024, held from September 10-12 at the Donald E Stephens Convention Center, will showcase a wide array of technological innovations transforming the label industry. This preview highlights advancements across flexible packaging, RFID, sustainability, and automation from numerous exhibitors. Companies ranging from 3M to Zhejiang Wanbaolong Adhesive Products will present new products and solutions, emphasizing efficiency, quality, and environmental responsibility.

title: Apple supplier Jabil to set up Rs 2,000 crore mfg unit in Tiruchirapalli
summary: Apple supplier Jabil Inc. is investing Rs 2,000 crore to establish an electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchirappalli, India, creating 5,000 jobs. This move signifies the expanding Apple supplier ecosystem in India, with Tamil Nadu emerging as a prominent electronics manufacturing hub. The investment is part of India's "China Plus One" strategy, drawing global players to diversify their manufacturing bases.

title: Apple's supplier Jabil to invest ₹2,000 cr in Tamil Nadu, aims to create 5,000 jobs
summary: Apple's contract manufacturer Jabil Inc. is set to invest ₹2,000 crore in a new electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchirapalli, Tamil Nadu, expecting to create 5,000 jobs. This makes Jabil the fourth Apple supplier in the state, joining Foxconn, Pegatron, and Tata Electronics, and was finalized during Tamil Nadu CM M.K. Stalin's U.S. visit. The investment contributes to Tamil Nadu's growing electronics exports, largely driven by iPhone shipments, positioning the state as India's electronics hub.

title: Jabil Inc to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy
summary: Jabil Inc, a key supplier to Apple and others, is set to invest Rs 2,000 crore in Trichy, Tamil Nadu. This significant investment is expected to create 5,000 jobs and foster a new electronics manufacturing cluster in the region, as announced by Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin.

title: Jabil Inc to pour Rs 2,000 Crore into new electronics manufacturing plant in Tiruchi
summary: Jabil Inc is committing Rs 2,000 crore to establish a new electronics manufacturing facility in Tiruchi, Tamil Nadu, a strategic investment expected to create approximately 5,000 jobs. This move is part of broader industrial development efforts in the region, with Jabil Inc. and Rockwell Automation collectively investing Rs 2,666 crore. The expansion reinforces Tamil Nadu's growing prominence as an electronics manufacturing hub.

title: 13 Black leaders in technology
summary: This article highlights the significant contributions of 13 influential Black leaders in the technology sector, acknowledging the industry's diversity challenges despite these innovations. It also recognizes seven historical Black tech leaders whose work continues to impact modern technology. The piece aims to inspire and encourage greater representation of Black professionals in tech by showcasing their achievements and ongoing efforts to foster diversity and inclusion.

title: HP Wolf Security Uncovers Evidence of Attackers Using AI to Generate Malware
summary: HP Wolf Security's latest Threat Insights Report reveals that attackers are using generative AI to create malicious code, including malware scripts distributed via malvertising campaigns. The report highlights a campaign targeting French speakers with AI-generated malware and notes that cybercriminals are increasingly embedding malware in SVG image files. These findings demonstrate how AI is lowering the barrier to entry for cybercriminals and diversifying attack methods.

title: Tackling Construction's Productivity Challenge: New HP Build Workspace Leads the Way
summary: HP has launched HP Build Workspace, a mobile cloud collaboration platform designed to enhance productivity in the construction industry. This platform aims to digitize workflows, reduce project lead times, and improve communication among stakeholders, addressing the industry's long-standing productivity issues exacerbated by labor shortages and manual processes. The new system integrates with HP SitePrint 3.0 and features the DesignJet T200/T600 Printer series 2025 edition, offering advanced AI capabilities for design, real-time feedback, and more efficient site layouts.

title: Broadcom: Semiconductors, M&A's, and Bold Leadership
summary: This article details Broadcom's journey from its origins in HP's division and Broadcom Corporation to becoming a global semiconductor giant through strategic mergers and acquisitions. It highlights CEO Hock Tan's leadership in expanding the company's product portfolio across semiconductors, software, and infrastructure solutions. The company's strategy involves targeting niche markets and utilizing a fabless design model to achieve market dominance.

title: Ingram Micro IPO, Revenue Details Go Live
summary: Ingram Micro has filed its registration statement with the SEC for a proposed initial public offering, with its common stock set to trade under the symbol "INGM" on the New York Stock Exchange. The filing reveals that Ingram Micro's net sales have declined over the past three years, from $54.4 billion in 2021 to $48 billion in 2023. Platinum Equity, which owns Ingram, will sell the common stock through its entity Imola JV Holdings, with the number of available shares and price range yet to be determined.

title: Desktop Metal Stockholders Approve Nano Dimension Merger
summary: Desktop Metal stockholders have approved the merger with Nano Dimension, a significant step in consolidating the additive manufacturing industry. This approval, backed by 96% of participating shares, is expected to lead to the transaction's close in Q4 2024, pending regulatory approvals. The merger, combined with Nano Dimension's pending acquisition of Markforged, aims to create a dominant force in the AM sector, leveraging Nano Dimension's financial reserves and comprehensive technology portfolio.

title: How AI gives Hershey an edge for high-stakes Halloween season
summary: Hershey is leveraging artificial intelligence to optimize its advertising during the high-stakes Halloween season, focusing on underserved markets rather than oversaturated ones. By working with Chalice Custom Algorithms, the company aims to allocate marketing budgets more effectively across platforms like Meta, YouTube, and The Trade Desk, especially given the influx of political advertising during an election year. This precision marketing strategy proved successful in a trial last Halloween, leading to increased sell-through with a key retailer.

title: After Vistra Stock’s (VST) 200%+ Move This Year, Is There More Potential Upside Before Q3 Earnings?
summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) stock has surged over 200% this year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 Utilities index. The article suggests further upside potential before Q3 2024 earnings, driven by increasing energy demand for AI data centers, strategic acquisitions expanding its nuclear and renewables capacity, and robust hedging strategies securing profitability. The company is also benefiting from a tech exodus to Texas, its home state, and federal production tax credits for nuclear power.

title: Ingram Micro IPO: Major tech product distributor debuts on NYSE today after pricing its stock within target range
summary: Ingram Micro Holding Corporation, a major behind-the-scenes tech product distributor with $48 billion in net sales last year, is making its initial public offering on the NYSE today, October 24, 2024, under the ticker symbol "INGM". The shares were priced at $22, within its target range, and the company is offering 18.6 million shares, raising approximately $255 million from its direct share sales. This marks a return to the public market for Ingram Micro, which was previously listed on the NYSE until 2016.

title: HP Envy 6155e review: a stylish printer made for Instant Ink
summary: The HP Envy 6155e is an attractive and affordable all-in-one printer designed for low-volume home use, especially beneficial when paired with HP's Instant Ink subscription service. While its raw ink cartridge costs are high and print speeds are moderate, the Instant Ink program significantly reduces the cost per page, making it competitive. Its sleek design and ease of setup are highlights, though it lacks an automatic document feeder and is not suited for heavy-duty printing.

title: Gail Evans Obituary - Charlotte, NC
summary: Gail Evans, a distinguished executive who held leadership roles at Eastman Kodak, Bank of America, HP, Microsoft, Mercer, and Disney, passed away on October 29, 2024, at the age of 65. Born in Rochester, NY, she diligently pursued education, earning degrees from Hobart William Smith, Nazareth University, and the University of Rochester. Evans was celebrated for her professional achievements and her dedication to her family, leaving behind a legacy of warmth, joy, and purpose.

title: HP Amplify AI
summary: HP has unveiled HP Amplify AI, a new persona-based program designed to enhance its partners' capabilities in driving AI outcomes. The initiative includes advanced AI-powered tools for sales and customer experiences, expanded global access to localized AI MasterClass training, and the global expansion of the HP Business Partner Program. These efforts aim to equip partners with the necessary knowledge and tools to excel in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

title: Staples teams with HP for large-format color printing
summary: Staples is partnering with HP to upgrade its U.S. stores with new HP DesignJet XL3800 printers, offering customers same-day access to large-format color prints such as posters, banners, and blueprints. This collaboration aims to make large-format printing more accessible for small businesses, retail owners, and consumers. The initiative builds on a decades-long partnership between Staples and HP, enhancing in-store printing services and efficiency.

title: Corsair Gaming, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:CRSR) market cap touched US$717m last week, benefiting both private equity firms who own 54% as well as institutions
summary: Corsair Gaming, Inc.'s market capitalization reached US$717 million last week, significantly benefiting private equity firms, which hold 54% ownership, and institutional investors, who own 30%. EagleTree Capital, LP is identified as the largest shareholder with 54% of outstanding shares. The article details the ownership structure, including insights into institutional, insider, and general public ownership, highlighting the influence of private equity.

title: Analysis: HP Reorganization Elevates Ex-Poly CEO’s Role In Growth Strategy
summary: HP's recent reorganization has significantly expanded the role of former Poly CEO Dave Shull, placing him at the helm of the newly formed HP Solutions group. This move integrates Workforce Solutions, Hybrid Systems (including Poly), and Advanced Compute Solutions, which focuses on AI development. The change is expected to bolster HP's growth strategy in key areas like AI software and collaboration tools, aligning with CEO Enrique Lores' Future Ready Plan.

title: HP’s Q4 FY24 Earnings: A Resilient Finish to a Challenging Year
summary: HP Inc. reported a resilient Q4 FY24 with $14.1 billion in revenue, a 1.7% year-over-year increase, driven by growth in Personal Systems and Printing segments. Despite a slight overall revenue decline for the fiscal year, the company demonstrated strong cash flow generation and shareholder returns, including a 5% dividend increase. HP is strategically focusing on high-growth areas like hybrid work, gaming, and industrial graphics, positioning itself for sustainable growth in FY25 amidst ongoing market challenges.

title: AMD Redefines Business Productivity with Third-Gen Ryzen™ AI PRO Processors: Powering the Future of Advanced AI Computing
summary: AMD has launched its third-generation Ryzen™ AI PRO 300 Series mobile processors, designed to enhance business productivity with advanced AI computing capabilities. These new processors feature the XDNA™ 2 architecture, delivering over 50 TOPS of AI processing power and are equipped with AMD PRO Technologies for enhanced security and IT management. The processors will power upcoming business laptops like the Lenovo ThinkPad T14s Gen 6 and the HP EliteBook X Next-Gen AI PC.

title: HP CEO Enrique Lores sells $4.99 million in stock
summary: HP Inc. CEO Enrique Lores recently sold 137,094 shares of company stock for approximately $4.99 million through a pre-established trading plan. This transaction occurred on December 9th, following his acquisition of 169,612 shares through stock option exercises and additional restricted stock unit transactions on December 7th. Despite the sale, Lores maintains significant direct and indirect holdings in HP, while the company focuses on AI integration, share buybacks, and dividend payments, amidst varying analyst ratings for its stock.

title: Lenovo, HP And Dell Are Betting Big On A PC Refresh In 2025 (So Is The Channel)
summary: Major PC manufacturers like Lenovo, HP, and Dell, along with their channel partners, are highly optimistic about a significant PC refresh in 2025. This anticipation is driven by several factors, including the Windows 10 end-of-life, the increasing demand for AI-capable PCs, and a large installed base of aging computers purchased during the COVID-19 pandemic. While some industry analysts predict the full surge of AI PC sales might extend into 2026, the consensus among these tech giants and solution providers points to 2025 as a pivotal year for PC market recovery, especially in the commercial sector.

title: HP Study Reveals $8.6bn Cost of Device Security Failures
summary: A study by HP Inc. reveals thatdevice security failures cost organizations an estimated $8.6 billion annually due to weak security practices, lost hardware, and audit failures. The research highlights significant gaps in platform security, procurement processes, and device management, exacerbated by remote work. Key issues include overlooked hardware security, widespread BIOS password sharing, delayed firmware updates, and inadequate verification of supplier security claims, leading to substantial financial impact and increased risk.

title: Eurostampa's Envelope Project Reimagines Nutella®'s Iconic Label Using Bria and HP Innovative Technology
summary: Eurostampa has collaborated with Bria, a visual generative AI solution, and HP to create 9,000 unique Nutella® labels for its 8th Envelope project. The project challenged students to reimagine Nutella's iconic label through various art movements using Gen-AI and advanced digital printing. Bria's platform, trained on licensed content for responsible AI, integrated with HP SmartStream Designer's 'HP Spark' technology, demonstrated the potential of AI in transforming personalized packaging and variable data printing while maintaining brand consistency.

title: HP: Supply Chain Security Failures are Costing Billions
summary: HP's report reveals that device security failures are costing organizations $10.9 billion annually, emphasizing critical vulnerabilities in procurement and supply chain processes. The article stresses the need for organizations to prioritize security when sourcing technology, with procurement teams playing a pivotal role in embedding cybersecurity requirements into supplier contracts and managing vendor risks. It highlights that balancing cost considerations with security is crucial to avoid significant financial and reputational damage from breaches.

title: ‘Synaptics plans to assemble 10% chips in India’
summary: US semiconductor company Synaptics Inc. plans to assemble and package 10% of its chips in India by 2027 to diversify its supply chain and reduce reliance on China. The company will use an OSAT facility in Hyderabad and partner with embed UR to develop edge AI solutions, aiming for the process to begin in late 2025 or early 2026. This move also addresses concerns about proposed US tariffs and the need for supply chain alternatives.

title: HP Smart Tank 7005 review: Low running costs are the main story for this otherwise fairly basic MFP
summary: The HP Smart Tank 7005 is a multifunction inkjet printer designed for home and micro offices, emphasizing extremely low running costs due to its refillable ink tanks. While it offers features like duplex printing and boasts exceptional value with included ink for thousands of pages, its performance is described as fairly basic, with slow print and scan speeds and disappointing scan quality, making it less ideal for busy office environments despite its affordability. The reviewer ultimately recommends the Canon MAXIFY GX2050 for a richer feature set and better results.

title: Amazon Accused of Mismanaging Millions of 401(k) Forfeiture Funds
summary: Amazon.com Inc. and its 401(k) plan administrative committee are facing a lawsuit for allegedly mismanaging millions in employee forfeiture funds. A participant, Cory Curtis, claims that Amazon used these funds to offset its own contributions rather than reducing administrative fees for plan participants between 2018 and 2023. The lawsuit highlights a growing trend of forfeiture-related litigation against large companies, although critics argue that such cases weaponize ERISA given Amazon's already low plan fees.

title: HP Inc. Unleashes New AI PCs At CES 2025, Wins Three Innovation Awards
summary: HP Inc. unveiled a new lineup of AI-powered PCs at CES 2025, winning three innovation awards for devices like the Omnibook Ultra Flip and its embedded AI Companion. The company is targeting various user types, from entry-level workers to on-the-go executives, with new models such as the OmniStudio X Series, EliteBook Ultra, and EliteBook X, all featuring enhanced NPUs and AI capabilities. HP emphasizes its commitment to providing tools that boost productivity, creativity, and connectivity for every user.

title: CES 2025: HP’s OMEN AI Casts a Spell on Gaming, Optimizing Performance with a Single Click
summary: HP announced new OMEN and HyperX gaming products at CES 2025, including the OMEN AI, the world's first AI-driven 1-click performance optimization tool. The OMEN MAX 16 is introduced as HP's most powerful gaming laptop, featuring NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs and enhanced thermal management. Additionally, HP unveiled the OMEN 32x Smart Gaming Monitor with built-in Google TV and the customizable HyperX Pulsefire Saga mice series.

title: HP finalizes $53 million in CHIPS Act money for Oregon expansion
summary: HP Inc. has finalized a $53 million award from the CHIPS Act to expand its research and manufacturing operations in Corvallis, Oregon. The funding will support innovation in microfluidics and micromechanics, adapting inkjet printing technology for drug discovery, and is expected to create 150 temporary construction jobs and 100 long-term positions. This award is part of the larger federal effort to revitalize the domestic semiconductor industry, though other CHIPS Act initiatives in Oregon have seen mixed results.

title: High-tech company with operations in Beaverton wins $105 million in CHIPS funding
summary: Analog Devices, Inc., a semiconductor company with operations in Beaverton, Camas, Washington, and Chelmsford, Massachusetts, is set to receive up to $105 million in federal CHIPS and Science Act funding. This investment is planned for facility expansions, particularly a "significant" one at its Beaverton plant, and is expected to create 500 new jobs across all sites while also focusing on environmental management and community partnerships. This funding is part of a broader federal effort to revitalize semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S., with other Oregon companies like HP, Intel, and Microchip Technologies also receiving CHIPS funds.

title: Medtronic appoints Renault executive Thierry Pieton as finance chief
summary: Medtronic (MDT.N) has announced the appointment of Thierry Pieton, Renault's (RENA.PA) finance chief, as its new Chief Financial Officer, effective March 3. Pieton will succeed interim CFO Gary Corona, following Karen Parkhill's departure to HP Inc (HPQ.N) last year.
