This move comes as Medtronic continues a turnaround strategy that included exiting its unprofitable ventilator product line.

title: HP Inc. plans move into Madwire’s Timberline building
summary: HP Inc. is reportedly relocating its operations to a building at 3405 S. Timberline Road in Fort Collins, formerly occupied by marketing firm Madwire since 2019. This move has been confirmed by Fort Collins economic-development officials. The article, however, is behind a subscriber paywall, limiting full access to further details.

title: HP Redefines High-Volume Digital Print with Two Game-Changing PageWide Presses
summary: HP has launched two new PageWide presses, the T4250 HDR and T500M HD, designed to enhance productivity and sustainability in high-volume digital printing. The T4250 HDR offers unmatched speed and quality for direct mail, commercial print, and publishing, doubling its predecessor's productivity. The T500M HD is tailored for high-volume monochrome book production, offering rapid turnarounds, minimal waste, and the ability to print on standard offset media.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Expands in Saudi Arabia with New AI R&D Center and Manufacturing Hub
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) is expanding its global presence by establishing a new AI R&D center and state-of-the-art manufacturing facility in Saudi Arabia. This move aims to cater to the increasing demand for computing devices and develop AI-driven solutions to enhance business efficiency and automation. The artificial intelligence center, based in Dhahran, will focus on AI-driven decision-making, data analytics, and robotic process automation, attracting top global and local AI talent.

title: HP Inks Humane Deal To Acquire Cosmos AI OS, Other Assets
summary: HP Inc. is set to acquire the AI-powered Cosmos platform and over 300 patents from Humane for $116 million, aiming to accelerate the development of AI-driven devices and services. The deal includes a team of Humane engineers joining HP to form HP IQ, an AI innovation lab. However, Humane's Ai Pin device is not part of the acquisition and will be discontinued.

title: HP Inc. Announces Acquisition of AI Capabilities from Humane to Enhance Intelligent Ecosystem
summary: HP Inc. is acquiring AI capabilities, including the AI platform Cosmos, technical talent, and over 300 patents, from Humane for $116 million to enhance its product innovation and create more intelligent experiences. This acquisition aims to transform HP into an "experience-led" company by integrating AI into new devices like PCs and printers and will establish HP IQ, a new AI innovation lab. The deal is expected to finalize by the end of February 2025.

title: HP just blew $116 million of your ink cartridge money to buy one of Silicon Valley's biggest flops
summary: HP has acquired the startup Humane for $116 million, a company whose Ai Pin product was a commercial flop and ridiculed by the public. Despite the Ai Pin selling fewer than 10,000 units and facing widespread criticism, HP's president of technology and innovation, Tuan Tran, stated that Humane's AI platform "Cosmos" and its engineers will accelerate HP's development of AI-powered devices like PCs and smart printers. The acquisition is viewed by the author as a tone-deaf move given Humane's controversial track record.

title: HP Labs Pioneering Tomorrows Technology Today | HP® Tech Takes
summary: HP Labs, the research and development arm of HP, has been a driving force in technological innovation since its establishment in 1966. This article explores its history, key milestones including the invention of LEDs and the LaserJet printer, and current focus areas such as AI, sustainable technology, and quantum computing. HP Labs continues to shape industries and define the digital age through its groundbreaking contributions.

title: HP Says Tariffs Could Weigh on Profitability This Year
summary: HP Inc. reported mixed first-quarter earnings, leading to a dip in after-hours trading. The company indicated that tariffs could negatively impact its profitability throughout the year, especially given the mature personal computer market.

title: HP Inc. was proactive about the impact of tariffs, and it may be paying off
summary: HP Inc. has proactively addressed potential tariff impacts by shifting manufacturing operations out of China and increasing inventory. The company expects over 90% of its North American products to be built outside China by the end of fiscal 2025. This strategy appears to be positively impacting its financial results, as evidenced by a jump in inventory in Q1 fiscal 2025 and beating revenue estimates.

title: HP Plans Up To 2,000 Layoffs To Balance Costs Amid Tariff Unknowns
summary: HP Inc. announced plans to lay off between 1,000 and 2,000 employees globally, aiming to save an additional $300 million as part of its "Future Ready" program. The company expects these cost-cutting measures, along with a restructured supply chain to reduce reliance on China for North American products, to help offset geopolitical uncertainties and fuel investments in growth areas. HP provided positive revenue and earnings guidance, reporting a 2.4 percent increase in quarterly revenue and a 5 percent rise in PC sales.

title: HP Accused of Blocking Rivals to Create Printer Ink Monopoly
summary: A class action lawsuit filed by Milberg alleges HP Inc. created a printer ink monopoly by using "Dynamic Security" firmware updates to block third-party cartridges, forcing consumers to buy more expensive HP-branded ink. This conduct led to higher prices and rendered non-HP cartridges unusable, prompting calls for an injunction and damages. Similar lawsuits and fines have been levied against HP in the past regarding its firmware updates.

title: Tech layoffs 2025 update: HP, Grubhub, Autodesk, Ibotta, are the latest to cut jobs and employees
summary: The tech sector has seen 18,397 job losses across 75 companies so far in 2025. Recent major announcements include HP planning to cut up to 2,000 workers due to a restructuring plan, and Grubhub laying off 500 employees following its acquisition by Winder. Autodesk and Ibotta have also announced job cuts, contributing to the ongoing trend of workforce reductions in the technology industry.

title: Trade Wars Come with a Price, and Investors May Already Paying It
summary: This article draws parallels between the McKinley Tariff Act of 1890 and present-day trade policies, arguing that tariffs, despite their stated goals, often lead to unintended consequences such as increased prices, reduced consumer confidence, and strained international relationships. Investors are advised to evaluate policies based on their actual effects, recognizing that tariffs act as a tax, create market volatility, and impact global trade dynamics. The author suggests that history shows markets prefer certainty, and past tariffs have ultimately led to real economic costs.

title: HP, Microchip announce thousands of layoffs
summary: Several manufacturers, including HP, Microchip Technology, Frito-Lay, CNH Industrial, and Rheem Manufacturing, have announced significant layoffs. These job cuts are attributed to corporate cost-cutting strategies and economic uncertainties, including the impact of new tariff policies. Microchip plans to cut 2,000 jobs to save up to $100 million, while HP aims to cut 1,000 to 2,000 workers to save $300 million as part of a broader restructuring.

title: HPE stock crashes as CEO weighs in on job cuts, earnings miss, and DOGE
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced significant job cuts and a material warning on full-year profits after its stock crashed due to disappointing server operating margins. CEO Antonio Neri attributed the miss to aggressive discounting and inventory devaluation, impacting HPE's financial outlook and leading analysts to reduce profit estimates. Rival Dell also saw a stock dip following HPE's announcement.

title: Should You Buy HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) For Its Upcoming Dividend?
summary: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) is set to go ex-dividend in four days, with a dividend payment of US$0.2894 per share. The company has a trailing yield of 3.9% and demonstrates a sustainable dividend policy, paying out 40% of earnings and 33% of free cash flow. HP has also shown consistent earnings growth, averaging 6.1% annually over the last five years, indicating a healthy prospect for continued dividend payments.

title: Thermal Inkjet Printing From: HP Inc.
summary: HP Inc. has released HP ThermaCore, a new thermal inkjet technology that aims to significantly improve coding and marking applications. This innovation boasts increased speed, precision, and throw distance, allowing manufacturers to enhance efficiency, meet global coding standards, and reduce operational costs. It also features a sustainable design, with the printhead made from 65% recycled plastic.

title: Introducing Breakthrough Innovations to Shape the Future of Work in Large Format Printing
summary: HP announced new innovations to address challenges in the large format printing industry, focusing on efficiency, versatility, and sustainability. Key introductions include the HP Latex R530 Printer for all-in-one rigid and flexible printing, the HP PrintOS Production Hub for streamlined workflows, and an industry-first AI solution for vectorization in HP Build Workspace to save time in construction documentation. These offerings are designed to help print service providers and construction companies improve operations and expand capabilities.

title: "Estamos saindo de um modelo globalizado para um regionalizado", diz CEO da HP - IT Forum
summary: Enrique Lores, CEO da HP Inc., announced at the HP Amplify conference that the global technology market is shifting from a globalized production model to a regionalized one, driven by geopolitical changes like proposed import tariffs. HP has been adapting its supply chain for three years, with 90% of its US-bound production moving out of China by the end of its fiscal year. Additionally, Lores highlighted the accelerating impact of AI, not only in the cloud but also at the edge, and HP's focus on transforming the work experience to boost productivity and employee satisfaction.

title: HP settles lawsuit after killing first responder's printers
summary: HP Inc. has settled a class-action lawsuit alleging it blocked customers from using third-party toner cartridges through firmware updates, refusing to pay any damages to resolve the case. One plaintiff, Mobile Emergency Housing Corp, providing shelters for disaster victims, had its HP printer rendered useless by a firmware update after using cheaper third-party toner. Although HP denies wrongdoing, it has agreed to continue making disclosures about its "Dynamic Security" and provide users the option to decline firmware updates that include it, highlighting the ongoing conflict between HP's revenue model and consumer choice.

title: HP, Inc. President and CEO Enrique Lores Receives Inaugural JUST Capital Lifetime Achievement Award
summary: Enrique Lores, President and CEO of HP Inc., has been honored with the inaugural JUST Capital Lifetime Achievement Award at the organization's 10th-anniversary gala. This award recognizes Lores' dedication to responsible business practices and HP's commitment to social responsibility, which has led to HP Inc. ranking second on Just Capital’s America’s Most Just Companies list. Under his leadership, HP has advanced its efforts in areas such as diversity, climate action, and digital equity, aligning business success with social impact.

title: Explore HP Labs’ Groundbreaking Innovations & Future Tech | HP® Tech Takes - Indonesia
summary: HP Labs has been a pioneering force in technology since its inception, consistently delivering industry-changing innovations from early personal computing to AI, sustainable technology, and quantum computing. The article explores HP Labs' journey, its significant achievements in printing, manufacturing, AI, and sustainability, and its future focus on AI-powered automation, sustainable hardware, quantum computing, and next-generation cybersecurity. It highlights how these innovations impact industries and society, particularly within Indonesia's dynamic and rapidly digitalizing market, empowering businesses and customers with cutting-edge solutions.

title: HP and Reincubate Partner to Deliver Personalized, NPU-Based, On-Device AI Capabilities
summary: HP Inc. and Reincubate have announced a multi-year strategic partnership to enhance on-device video and video conferencing experiences using NPU-based AI capabilities. This collaboration will leverage Reincubate's AI video technology to deliver secure, private, and efficient digital collaboration features on HP's next-generation AI PCs. The AI processing will run locally on the NPU, ensuring low latency, privacy, and improved battery life for users.

title: Connection Wins Two HP Inc. Partner of the Year Awards
summary: Connection (PC Connection, Inc.; NASDAQ: CNXN) has been honored with two HP Partner of the Year Awards: HP U.S. Commercial Supplies Partner of the Year and HP U.S. Print Hardware NSP of the Year. These awards recognize Connection's outstanding performance, collaboration, and strategic growth with HP. The company attributes this success to its strong partnership with HP and a shared focus on customer service and innovation in technology solutions.

title: HP settles computer false discount class action lawsuit for $4 million
summary: HP Inc. has agreed to a $4 million settlement in a class action lawsuit alleging misleading discount advertising on its website for various computer products. Consumers who purchased discounted HP desktop computers, laptops, mice, or keyboards directly from HP's website between June 5, 2021, and October 28, 2024, may be eligible for a cash payment of $10 to $100 per item. The deadline to submit a claim is June 9, 2025, and payments are expected to be distributed after final approval in August 2025.

title: HP Inc reinvents their regional cash flow forecasting with HSBC | Insights | HSBC
summary: HP Inc collaborated with HSBC to automate its regional cash flow forecasting, transitioning from manual, error-prone processes to HSBC’s Cash Flow Forecasting (CFF) tool within HSBCnet. This has significantly reduced man-hours, improved forecast accuracy, and provided real-time visibility into cash positions across five Asia Pacific markets, with plans for further expansion. The solution has led to substantial efficiency gains and received awards for its innovative approach in cash flow management.

title: Marcos Razon, SVP and Division President of HP Lifecycle Services and Customer Support, HP Inc.
summary: This page profiles Marcos Razon, the SVP and Division President of HP Lifecycle Services and Customer Support at HP Inc. It highlights his role in overseeing strategy, P&L, and a services portfolio including PC and Print Support, Configuration, and Deployment, ensuring global support across 180+ countries. The article also mentions his 30 years of experience at HP and includes a link to an article authored by him.

title: HP imprime forte investimento no Brasil. Saiba o papel do País na evolução da companhia
summary: HP is making significant investments in Brazil, leveraging the country's operation as fundamental for its global business, which had a revenue of US$53.6 billion. The company's evolution in Brazil is driven by the adoption of AI in products, a strong focus on sustainability, and an increase in exports from local factories. Brazil plays a strategic role in HP's global supply chain and has shown double-digit growth in PC and printer sales, surpassing global averages.

title: Technology Alliances
summary: Absolute Software offers "Technology Alliances" that integrate its self-healing security solutions with leading cybersecurity and IT platforms. These alliances aim to ensure applications remain operational, protected, and secure by leveraging Absolute's Application Resilience and Persistence Technology to counter security gaps and software vulnerabilities. Partners benefit from enhanced security offerings, continuous protection against cyber threats, and seamless integration capabilities.

title: 5 Best Laptops for Online College Classes in 2024
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to selecting the best laptops for online college classes in 2025, focusing on HP models. It details key features like performance, battery life, and portability, and recommends specific HP laptops for overall use, high performance, budget constraints, 2-in-1 versatility, and portability, considering different academic majors.

title: HP Holiday Gift Guide 2025: Top Tech Gifts for Everyone | HP® Tech Takes SEO
summary: HP's 2025 Holiday Gift Guide features a wide range of tech products, including desktops, laptops, printers, monitors, and accessories, designed to suit various needs and budgets. The guide highlights specific HP and OMEN models, detailing their features and ideal users, from serious gamers and creative professionals to students and home office workers. It also offers shopping tips, such as checking for deals, protecting purchases with Care Packs, and personalizing gifts based on the recipient's preferences.

title: $4M HP.com misleading discounts class action settlement
summary: HP has agreed to a $4 million class action settlement to resolve claims of misleading discounts on its website. Consumers who purchased certain HP desktop computers, laptops, mice, and keyboards at a discount on HP.com between June 5, 2021, and October 28, 2024, are eligible to file a claim. Individual awards range from $10 to $100, subject to pro rata adjustments, and the deadline to file a claim is June 9, 2025.

title: HP Multi Jet Fusion: Revolutionizing the drone industry with 3D printed drones
summary: This article highlights how HP Multi Jet Fusion (MJF) technology is transforming the drone industry through enhanced customization, efficiency, and rapid prototyping. It showcases three companies—Inverto Earth, Frameworks Robotics, and UAV Works—that leveraged HP MJF to innovate sustainable mangrove planting drones, modular underwater vehicles, and high-performance aerial systems. The technology offers benefits such as design freedom, durability, on-demand production, and weight optimization, proving to be a game-changer for both UAVs and UUVs.

title: Elliott Management takes a stake in Hewlett Packard Enterprise. How the firm may create value
summary: Elliott Management has taken a $1.5 billion stake in Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), seeing significant undervaluation due to poor execution and a pending acquisition of Juniper Networks. The activist investor believes its involvement, potentially through board representation, can restore shareholder confidence and ensure value creation regardless of the Juniper acquisition's outcome, leveraging its strong track record in tech activism. HPE's diverse portfolio, especially its high-multiple networking business, positions it favorably against competitors and potential geopolitical headwinds.

title: Capital Group, Dimensional Top Active ETF Inflows Amid Volatility
summary: During a period of market volatility in early April, investors funneled $2.4 billion into US large-cap active ETFs, with Capital Group leading the inflows. The firm's Dividend Value ETF alone attracted about $648 million, while Dimensional Fund Advisors also saw significant inflows. Despite the active ETF growth, passive US large-cap ETFs still attracted a much larger $20.2 billion during the same timeframe.

title: AstroNova to launch digital label presses and direct-to-package printer at FESPA 2025
summary: AstroNova plans to introduce two new digital label presses, the QL-425 and QL-435, and a direct-to-package printer, the VP-800, at FESPA Global Print Expo 2025. These new offerings are designed to provide speed, flexibility, and cost-efficiency for the light to medium production segment, targeting label converters, brand-owners, and print service providers. The company emphasizes greater control, increased uptime, and lower operating costs through advanced hardware and intelligent automation.

title: MediaTek and HP bring educational Chromebooks to EDIX Tokyo 2025
summary: MediaTek and HP are showcasing the new HP Fortis Flip G1m 11 Chromebook, powered by the MediaTek Kompanio 520 processor, at EDIX Tokyo 2025. This collaboration aims to provide K-12 students in Japan with powerful, efficient, and versatile educational tools, supporting the country's GIGA program. The Chromebook offers enhanced performance, extended battery life, and multitasking capabilities designed to improve digital learning experiences.

title: Scuderia Ferrari and HP Fuse Technology and Design with Special Livery for Miami Grand Prix
summary: Scuderia Ferrari and HP have unveiled a special co-designed livery for the Miami Grand Prix, celebrating the first year of their title partnership. This innovative design combines Ferrari red with HP's white and electric blue, utilizing new co-engineered technologies for visual and performance enhancements, including lighter and thinner car wraps. The collaboration also extends to optimizing Ferrari's working environment in Maranello and at the track with HP technology.

title: NETGEAR Opens Software Development Center in Chennai
summary: NETGEAR Inc. has announced the opening of a new software development center in Chennai, India, to enhance its AI and cloud-based solutions for advanced business connectivity. This initiative is part of a larger R&D investment aimed at developing next-generation networking solutions that prioritize simplicity, reliability, and cost-effectiveness. The Chennai center was established through the acquisition of talent and IP assets from local cloud software company VAAG Systems, whose team will focus on integrating AI for end-to-end network troubleshooting.

title: HP Inc.: A Bet On Efficiency, Innovation, And Aggressive Capital Return (NYSE:HPQ)
summary: HP Inc. is refocusing on high-margin products and corporate services, leveraging AI, premium PCs, and cloud solutions to improve profitability. Despite a stagnant market, the company generates strong free cash flow and returns capital to shareholders through buybacks and dividends, making it a stable investment. HPQ is currently undervalued compared to historical and peer metrics, presenting a buying opportunity with a fair valuation of $30.16 and a potential 20.72% total return, despite risks like US tariff policies and market competition.

title: Dye Sublimation Printing Market Is Now Valued at $16 Billion
summary: The global dye sublimation printing market is projected to reach $16 billion, driven by increasing demand for customized apparel and promotional merchandise, especially from small businesses and e-commerce. The market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 11.3% to $28.0 billion by 2030, with textile manufacturing advancements in the Asia Pacific region and the development of eco-friendly, hybrid printing technologies being key growth factors. Challenges exist with natural fibers, but innovation in inks, printers, and direct-to-fabric techniques continue to expand market applications.

title: HP Inc: Undervalued, But Don't Expect Too Much Growth
summary: HP Inc. is considered a market leader in laptops and printers, yet faces slow growth prospects in these industries. Despite stagnant revenue and net income over the past five years, the company's valuation appears attractive, offering decent upside potential due to its stable recurring revenue from high-margin ink sales and diversified global presence. The author rates HP as a "BUY" for value-oriented investors seeking lower risk and steady returns through dividends, though future growth is expected to be very modest.

title: NVIDIA Launches AI-First DGX Personal Computing Systems With Global Computer Makers
summary: NVIDIA has announced new DGX Spark and DGX Station personal AI supercomputers, built by leading manufacturers like Acer, ASUS, Dell Technologies, GIGABYTE, HP, Lenovo, and MSI. These systems are designed to offer unprecedented AI performance and efficiency for developers, researchers, and data scientists, addressing the growing demand for robust AI computing solutions that can handle agentic AI systems. Powered by NVIDIA Grace Blackwell platforms, they enable prototyping, fine-tuning, and inference of models from desktop to data center, with DGX Spark available in July and DGX Station later this year.

title: HPQ Earnings: All you need to know about HP’s Q2 2025 earnings results
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) reported its second-quarter 2025 earnings, with net revenue up 3.3% year-over-year to $13.2 billion, beating estimates, while net earnings and GAAP/adjusted EPS decreased. The company lowered its fiscal year 2025 outlook, leading to a 15% stock plunge in aftermarket hours.

title: Leading Global Manufacturer Requests HPQ’s First Batch of Fumed Silica for Evaluation
summary: HPQ Silicon Inc. announced that a leading global fumed silica manufacturer has requested early samples of their fumed silica, generated using HPQ's proprietary Fumed Silica Reactor (FSR) Pilot Plant. This early interest follows independent validation of HPQ Silica Polvere Inc.'s fumed silica production. The collaboration aims to accelerate the pilot plant program by enhancing product-grade purity and reaching a targeted production capacity of 50 tonnes per year, ultimately accelerating market entry for HPQ's scalable, high-purity fumed silica solution.

title: HP Inc caiu mais de 8% na quinta-feira após cortar previsão de lucro e reforçar saída da China
summary: As ações da HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) e da BDR (BOV:HPQB34) caíram significativamente após a empresa divulgar uma previsão de lucro abaixo do esperado e reduzir sua projeção anual, citando tarifas elevadas e uma economia mais fraca. A HP Inc. também confirmou a aceleração de sua retirada da China, com a produção sendo realocada para países como Vietnã, Índia, México e Estados Unidos para mitigar os impactos tarifários.

title: Watch These HP Price Levels as Stock Tumbles After Company Cuts Outlook Due to Tariffs
summary: HP (HPQ) shares tumbled after the company missed profit expectations and issued a weak outlook, attributing this to increased tariff costs. The stock had already broken down from a rising wedge pattern before the announcement. Investors should monitor key support levels at $25, $23, and $21, and a key overhead resistance level near $29.

title: HP Inc. Q2 Earnings: Why I Am Not Buying After The Dip (NYSE:HPQ)
summary: HP Inc.'s Q2 FY 2025 earnings fell short of expectations, impacted by tariffs and a weak printing segment, despite growth in personal systems. While management is working to mitigate tariff effects and improve cost savings, the author remains cautious due to potential tariff-related volatility, limited margin, and high SG&A expenses. Despite an attractive valuation, the author suggests selling put options as a potentially safer long strategy than directly buying HPQ shares.

title: Dell Technologies vs. HPQ: Which PC Stock Has More Growth Potential?
summary: This article compares Dell Technologies (DELL) and HP (HPQ) as investments in the growing PC market, which is projected to reach $344.13 billion by 2030. It highlights DELL's strong AI infrastructure, enterprise focus, and robust earnings outlook, especially with its AI PCs and partnerships with NVIDIA and Microsoft. HPQ is also expanding its AI PC portfolio but faces risks from its heavy reliance on Chinese manufacturing amid potential tariff escalations.

title: HP Delivers Exceptional Experiences and Value through Expanded Portfolio of Services and Solutions
summary: HP has expanded its portfolio of services and solutions for channel partners to address the evolving demands of hybrid work environments and the increasing complexity of IT management. These offerings include new managed device and collaboration services, enhanced support for print, out-of-band diagnosis capabilities, security advisory services, and carbon neutral computing options. HP is also providing improved tools and training to empower partners to better serve their customers and drive business growth.

title: Lenovo Vs. HP Vs. Dell PC Sales Showdown In 2025 So Far
summary: The article details the 2025 PC sales performance of Lenovo, HP, and Dell, highlighting a PC refresh driven by commercial buyers ahead of the Windows 11 migration and potential tariff impacts. While IDC projects overall market growth, consumer sales remain challenged compared to strong commercial demand. The three companies show varying revenue and market share, with projections for continued PC growth in the coming quarters.

title: John Young, headed HP in 1980s, dies at 93
summary: John A. Young, the former CEO of Hewlett-Packard who led the company's significant growth from 1978 to 1992, has died at 93. He transformed HP from a test and measurement equipment pioneer into a global computing powerhouse, increasing annual revenue from $1.3 billion to $16 billion. Young also chaired President Reagan’s Commission on Industrial Competitiveness and was known for his love of the outdoors and his amiable personality.

title: HP’s New Device Management Tools: ‘Better Customer Experience’ And ‘Better Revenue For Partner’
summary: HP Inc. is introducing new device management tools for PCs, print, and collaboration, aimed at improving customer experience and partner profitability. These tools allow solution providers to proactively diagnose and fix PC problems, including remote remediation for unbootable devices using Intel chips. HP's Managed Device Services for PCs and Premium+ Support for printers are designed to provide partners with more strategic customer relationships and increased revenue opportunities through enhanced service offerings.

title: HP Inc. Declares Cash Dividend of $0.2894 Per Share for Fiscal Year 2025
summary: HP Inc. has announced a cash dividend of $0.2894 per share for its common stock, marking the fourth dividend for its fiscal year 2025. This dividend is payable on October 1, 2025, to shareholders of record as of September 10, 2025. The company highlights its commitment to returning value to shareholders and its global leadership in technology.

title: HP Dimension with Google Beam Takes Virtual Collaboration to the Next Level at InfoComm 2025
summary: HP Inc. unveiled HP Dimension with Google Beam at InfoComm 2025, an AI-powered 3D video communication solution designed to enhance virtual collaboration by replicating in-person experiences without wearables. Developed with Google, this technology aims to create more authentic connections in distributed workforces through features like 3D imaging, natural eye contact, and spatial audio. Additionally, HP introduced Poly Studio A2 Audio Solutions, providing advanced audio for various meeting room sizes, with both products becoming available to customers in late 2025.

title: The future of agriculture: How HP's AI-enabled technology is transforming the way farmers work
summary: HP's AI-enabled technology is transforming agriculture by empowering startups like Amini, which uses satellite imagery and AI to provide real-time insights to African farmers. Amini leverages HP Z workstations to process vast amounts of data locally, enabling quick experimentation and delivering actionable intelligence for better crop management and increased yields. This partnership allows farmers to mitigate potential crop health issues and adapt to changing weather patterns, significantly boosting productivity and prosperity in data-scarce regions.

title: Forget Star Trek, HP Just Made 3D Hologram Calls A Reality With Google Beam
summary: HP has partnered with Google to launch the HP Dimension with Google Beam, an AI-powered 3D video communications solution designed to make virtual meetings feel more in-person. This system, which evolved from Project Starline, uses volumetric video and specialized light field displays to create lifelike 3D representations of participants without requiring headsets. While promising enhanced engagement and focus, the technology comes with a significant cost, priced at $25,000 per unit plus separate licensing fees for Google Beam.

title: Google's First 'Beam' Videoconferencing Device is 'HP Dimension', Coming Late 2025 at $25,000
summary: HP has unveiled 'Dimension', its first hardware offering based on Google Beam's 3D videoconferencing platform, targeting enterprise clients at a price of $25,000. The device, which uses six cameras and AI to create realistic 3D video on a 65-inch light field display, aims to enhance human connection in virtual meetings. While marketed as a light-field display, some commentators suggest it's a sophisticated auto-stereoscopic system with advanced rendering rather than a true light-field technology.

title: HP Inc.: Cheap Valuation Driven By Harsher Printing Declines And Weaker PC Margins (Upgrade)
summary: HP Inc. is facing macroeconomic challenges, with its print division experiencing declines and its PC segment showing modest growth but weakening margins. Despite a seemingly attractive valuation with a near 5% yield and an 11x free cash flow multiple, providing some downside protection, near-term upside catalysts are limited. The author is upgrading HP to neutral, citing cheap valuation as a guard against significant drops, but cautions about long-term risks in the printing business and ongoing margin pressures.

title: An Intel-HP Collaboration Delivers Next-Gen AI PCs
summary: Intel and HP have collaborated to deliver next-generation AI PCs, with Intel optimizing hardware and software for AI workloads on HP's new business laptops. This partnership led to enhanced performance for various AI-powered applications, some of which are now available on Windows-based PCs for the first time. The collaboration underscores a significant shift towards AI-first computing, aiming to provide tangible value and improved user experiences.

title: AI Implementation Roadmap: From Infrastructure to Services to Applications
summary: This article outlines a comprehensive, six-phase roadmap for successful AI implementation in businesses, addressing common challenges and highlighting key success factors. It covers strategic alignment, infrastructure design, data governance, model development, deployment through MLOps, and long-term governance and ethics. The guide emphasizes the importance of a structured approach to overcome the high failure rate of AI projects and achieve significant business value.

title: HP Brings On US Steel Exec To Replace Departing Accounting Chief
summary: HP Inc. announced that Manpreet Grewal, formerly of U.S. Steel, will replace Stephanie Liebman as its chief accounting officer, effective July 14. Liebman is departing in September for an "outside opportunity" after 22 years at HP. Grewal comes with a background in navigating significant corporate acquisitions, and his appointment occurs as HP is dramatically restructuring its North American supply chain to reduce reliance on China.

title: Millions of Printers Exposed to Hacking Due to New Vulnerabilities
summary: New research from Rapid7 has uncovered eight vulnerabilities affecting 748 models of multifunction printers from five different vendors, including Brother, FUJIFILM Business Innovation, Ricoh, Konica Minolta, and Toshiba Tec. The most critical vulnerability, CVE-2024-51978, allows for authentication bypass and could lead to the leakage of device serial numbers and administrator passwords. Cybersecurity experts emphasize that printers are often overlooked security risks, especially in remote work environments, and urge organizations to prioritize patching and implement stronger IoT security measures to prevent lateral movement by attackers and protect sensitive data.

title: Firestorm, HP partner to advance expeditionary manufacturing
summary: Firestorm Labs, Inc. has secured exclusive distribution rights from HP Inc. for its mobile Multi Jet Fusion (MJF) 3D printing technologies, making Firestorm the sole provider of HP’s high-performance 3D printers in mobile and field-deployable environments. This partnership aims to advance expeditionary manufacturing through Firestorm's xCell system, a scalable manufacturing unit housed in expandable containers that can operate in remote locations. The collaboration also focuses on workforce development, creating new high-tech jobs in system operations, field deployment, and drone manufacturing.

title: Marsh McLennan appoints Bruce Broussard to its Board of Directors
summary: Marsh McLennan has appointed Bruce Broussard to its Board of Directors, effective immediately, bringing the total number of directors to 12. Broussard, formerly CEO of Humana and McKesson Specialty/US Oncology, adds extensive experience in the insurance and healthcare sectors. His appointment is expected to enhance the Board's perspective on various critical issues.

title: DOL Issues Brief Defending Employer Forfeiture Practices
summary: The Department of Labor (DOL) has filed an amicus brief supporting plan sponsors in a lawsuit concerning forfeiture practices within 401(k) plans. This brief urges the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals to uphold a district court decision in _Hutchins v. HP, Inc._, where a participant alleged HP misused forfeited funds. The DOL's stance emphasizes that forfeiture practices are permissible under the Internal Revenue Code and ERISA, and that challenging them creates an uncertain landscape for retirement plans.

title: DOL Backs Companies on 401(k) Plan Forfeiture Complaints
summary: The Department of Labor (DOL) has sided with HP Inc. in a legal dispute concerning the management of forfeited funds within its 401(k) retirement plan. The DOL's amicus brief stated that using forfeited employer contributions to satisfy matching obligations, as alleged by plaintiff Paul Hutchins, does not violate ERISA, distinguishing fiduciary responsibilities from "settlor" decisions related to plan design and funding. This position is significant as it supports companies facing over 50 similar complaints this year and aligns with the DOL's stated goal of limiting ERISA litigation.

title: Take Care Before Diving Into The Deep End On HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ)
summary: HP Inc.'s current P/E ratio of 9.6x is significantly lower than the market average in the United States, which could indicate a potential buying opportunity. However, this low P/E is likely due to the company's recent 12% decline in earnings, with a 52% drop over the last three years. While analysts project a 9.0% annual growth for HP over the next three years, similar to the broader market, investors seem unconvinced of the company's ability to meet these expectations, leading to the suppressed P/E ratio.

title: The HP All-In Plan fixes everything annoying about owning a printer
summary: The HP All-In Plan is a subscription service designed to eliminate common frustrations associated with printer ownership by offering a new printer, automatic ink deliveries, technical support, and replacement coverage for a monthly fee starting at $7.99. This plan, which includes a 30-day risk-free trial, aims to provide a hassle-free printing experience for various user needs, from occasional use to high-volume business tasks. Subscribers can choose different printer models and page allowances, with commitments typically lasting two to three years.

title: EXCLUSIVE: HP Is Launching an Ad Business With Laptop-Targeted Ads and a Streaming Service
summary: HP is reportedly launching an ad network called HP Media Network, aiming to capture a share of advertising budgets. The tech manufacturer, a major PC seller, plans to leverage its substantial global audience of 830 million people across 100 million devices, including 160 million U.S. users, to offer targeted ads. This move comes as HP seeks new revenue streams, despite facing competition from Apple in the PC market.

title: Only 36% of IT Teams Apply Printer Firmware Updates Promptly, Leaving Devices Vulnerable
summary: A new report by HP Wolf Security, "Securing the Print Estate: A Proactive Lifecycle Approach to Cyber Resilience," reveals critical security gaps in printer management. Only 36% of IT and security decision-makers promptly apply firmware updates to printers, despite spending 3.5 hours per printer monthly on security tasks. This oversight, along with other issues across the printer's lifecycle, leaves organizations vulnerable to cyber threats, data exfiltration, and physical risks.

title: UK court to rule on how much HP owed by late Mike Lynch's estate over Autonomy deal
summary: London's High Court is set to rule on the amount Hewlett Packard is owed by the estate of late Autonomy co-founder Mike Lynch and his former business partner following HP's 2011 acquisition of Autonomy. HP accused Lynch and former CFO Sushovan Hussain of inflating Autonomy's value, leading to an $8.8 billion write-down and a $5 billion lawsuit, with a previous ruling in HP's favor in 2022. The case proceeds against Lynch's estate after his death in a yacht sinking, with the High Court now determining damages.

title: CDW cuts more workers in fourth round of layoffs over 2 years
summary: Computer reseller CDW recently laid off approximately 300 workers in its fourth round of cutbacks in just over two years, bringing the total estimated job reductions to around 1,500. This frequent workforce realignment has raised concerns about employee morale and the company's ability to retain and attract talent, despite the company's first-quarter sales and profit showing increases from the prior year. Analyst Keith Housum suggests these layoffs are primarily for skill-set realignment rather than a broad workforce reduction.

title: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) is largely controlled by institutional shareholders who own 84% of the company
summary: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) is largely controlled by institutional shareholders, who own 84% of the company. Institutional ownership typically implies greater credibility in the investment community due to their significant resources and liquidity. While The Vanguard Group, BlackRock, Inc., and State Street Global Advisors, Inc. are major shareholders, no single entity has dominant control, as the top 16 investors collectively account for 51% ownership.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Announces Fourth Dividend Payment of Fiscal Year 2025
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) has declared its fourth dividend payment for fiscal year 2025, amounting to $0.2894 per share. This dividend will be payable on October 1 to shareholders of record as of September 10. The company also repurchased approximately 3 million shares for $100 million and returned a total of $400 million to shareholders during Q2 FY2025 through dividends and buybacks.

title: Top 10: Influential Supply Chain Leaders
summary: This article identifies the top 10 most influential supply chain leaders globally, highlighting their roles in transforming their respective companies. The list includes executives from major corporations like HP, Target, Nestlé, UPS, and Maersk.

title: AIG taps financial sector veteran as chief digital officer

Hallworth, previously chief data and analytics officer at HP, will lead AIG's digital, data, and generative AI initiatives, reporting directly to CEO Peter Zaffino from January 1st. This appointment is part of AIG's ongoing AIG Next modernization program, which has already achieved significant cost savings and streamlined operations through technology adoption, including generative AI.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Launches New Gaming Desktops, Headsets & Mics at Level Reforge LA
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) unveiled new gaming desktops, headsets, and microphones at its Level Reforge event in Los Angeles on August 14. The OMEN MAX 45L and OMEN 35L Gaming Desktops, including a Stealth Edition, were introduced, alongside expanded OMEN AI capabilities for popular titles. Additionally, HyperX launched the Cloud Alpha 2 Wireless headset and the FlipCast microphone.

title: Executives on the Move at AIG, FM, CNA
summary: American International Group Inc. (AIG) has appointed Scott Hallworth as chief digital officer, effective September 1, to lead their digital, data, and GenAI strategy. CNA Financial Corp. announced several leadership changes, including David Haas as President Global Specialty, Michael Nardiello as President Global Property and Casualty, and Song Kim as President Global Commercial Industry Segments.

title: HP CEO Enrique Lores Sees ‘Good News For The Channel’ With PC Refresh Into 2026
summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores anticipates continued growth for the PC market and channel, extending into fiscal year 2026. This optimism is driven by the ongoing Windows 11 refresh, which is still incomplete, and the increasing adoption of AI PCs, which now represent 25% of personal systems sales. HP reported a 3% overall sales increase last quarter, with strong growth in both consumer and commercial PC segments, while printer sales saw a decline.

title: HP Expects Growing Demand for AI-Enabled Personal Computers to Continue
summary: HP announced that its third-quarter sales were bolstered by increasing demand for AI-enabled personal computers, a trend its CEO, Enrique Lores, anticipates will persist into next year. The company saw double-digit quarter-over-quarter growth in AI PC sales, particularly from commercial clients seeking productivity advantages. This strong performance contributed to HP exceeding revenue expectations.

title: 5 Revealing Analyst Questions From HP’s Q2 Earnings Call
summary: HP's Q2 earnings call revealed several key insights through analyst questions. The company exceeded revenue expectations, driven by strong personal systems performance, particularly AI-powered PCs and commercial premium products. While tariffs were largely mitigated, analysts pressed on the sustainability of PC market strength, printer margin volatility, and the price premium of AI PCs.

title: HP showcases breakthrough orthotics and prosthetics case studies
summary: HP is highlighting new advancements in orthotics and prosthetics, including a collaboration with the Limb Kind Foundation to provide 3D-printed pediatric prosthetics in underserved communities and the first in-house 3D-printed transtibial prosthetic socket for VA Puget Sound. The company is also announcing the general availability of its new HP 3D HR PA 11 Gen2 material, designed for high reusability and lower part costs in O&P applications. These initiatives are being showcased at the AOPA National Assembly, demonstrating HP's commitment to digital manufacturing in personalized healthcare.

title: 3D Printing Market to Reach USD 210.5 Billion by 2035 | Growing at a CAGR of 24.25% - Vantage Market Research
summary: The 3D Printing Market is projected to grow from USD 19.33 billion in 2024 to USD 210.5 billion by 2035, exhibiting a robust CAGR of 24.25%. This growth is driven by advancements in additive manufacturing, rising adoption across various industries like aerospace, automotive, and healthcare, and increased demand for customized and on-demand production. North America currently leads the market, while Asia Pacific is expected to show the fastest growth, propelled by Industry 4.0 initiatives and expanding manufacturing capacities.

title: HP sees earlier than expected spike in AI PC shipments
summary: HP has seen a faster-than-anticipated increase in AI PC shipments, with these premium units making up one-quarter of its Q3 2025 shipments, a threshold CFO Karen Parkhill expected later in the year. Parkhill noted that CIOs are refreshing their installed base with AI PCs to future-proof their operations, anticipating that AI-optimized PCs will constitute half of HP's PC business within the next two years. The broader PC market recovery, driven by aging enterprise devices and Microsoft's Windows 10 end-of-support deadline, is further fueling this adoption, despite earlier slowdowns in AI PC uptake.

title: 500+ Expert Sessions: Trimble Dimensions 2025 to Showcase AI-Powered Construction Tech Revolution

The conference will feature over 500 expert-led sessions, focusing on AI integrations and connecting physical and digital worlds in AEC and geospatial workflows. title: Industry trends point to opportunity for ITAD firms
summary: Technology companies like Hewlett Packard Enterprise are reporting significant infrastructure upgrades in servers, networking gear, and storage driven by operating system refreshes and AI adoption. This trend is leading to a growing volume of used equipment entering secondary markets, creating substantial opportunities for ITAD (IT Asset Disposition) firms and recyclers. While OEMs like HPE are processing some end-of-lease returns internally, the overall influx of decommissioned hardware points to a sustained increase in supply for the ITAD industry.

title: Undeniably Clutch: The OMEN 35L VALORANT Limited Edition Has Arrived
summary: HP has launched the OMEN 35L VALORANT Limited Edition desktop PC, created in collaboration with Riot Games and designed for VALORANT fans. This limited-edition desktop features VALORANT-themed aesthetics and boasts high-performance components, including an Intel Core i7-14700F processor and NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 graphics. It is available for a starting price of $1,889.99, aiming to provide a personalized and powerful gaming experience for the VALORANT community.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Downgraded to “In Line” as Analysts See Limited Upside
summary: Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani downgraded HP Inc. (HPQ) from "Outperform" to "In Line" with a $29 price target, citing limited upside potential. The downgrade is due to a lack of clear growth drivers for earnings and cash flow, despite anticipated growth in the PC market. Challenges like printing division headwinds, increased competition from Dell, and temporary PC strength driven by tariff concerns also factored into the decision.

title: VOO ETF Analysis: Vanguard S&P 500 Outlook, Price $949 Fair Value
summary: The VOO ETF has shown an 18% return in the past year and trades at a P/E ratio of 9.4x, suggesting undervaluation with a fair value near $949 per share. While the ETF benefits from potential Fed rate cuts and fiscal stimulus, it faces vulnerability from inflation, particularly a 2.9% year-over-year CPI, and tariff-driven shocks affecting its heavily weighted technology and consumer sectors. Despite these risks and stretched valuations (S&P 500 P/B of 5.3x), VOO is rated a Buy due to its diversification, low expense ratio, and long-term compounding benefits.

title: Announcing Trimble Dimensions 2025: Engage the Future of Work Now
summary: Trimble (Nasdaq: TRMB) has announced its flagship user conference, Trimble Dimensions 2025, will take place from November 10-12, 2025, in Las Vegas. The event will focus on connecting physical and digital worlds to transform industries, offering over 500 expert-led sessions, live demos, and workshops. It will also feature a keynote from CEO Rob Painter, a presentation by RFK Racing co-owner Brad Keselowski, and showcase emerging companies in the Trimble 0-60 Challenge and Construction Startup Competition Pitch Day.

title: Super Micro Computer Stock Prediction: Where Analysts See the Stock Going by 2028
summary: Analysts are divided on the future trajectory of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) stock, which has seen a significant run-up due to strong demand for AI-optimized servers. While the average analyst price target suggests a potential 126% upside, the forecasts show a wide spread, indicating weak conviction. The company's fundamentals are solid with projected 30% annual revenue growth, but thin margins and intense competition pose risks.

The company, which employs about 2,500 people in Israel, had previously laid off employees in January 2024 and October 2022.

The Productivity Kit upgrades existing Latex FS50s and 60s to FS70-level performance, boosting speed and efficiency, while the PrintOS Production Hub software receives continuous updates for streamlined workflows and new functionality.

title: TD Africa, HP Strengthen Partnership, Eye Expansion across Africa
summary: TD Africa and HP have strengthened their partnership to enhance technology accessibility across Africa. Both companies expressed optimism about delivering value to customers and innovating to meet the continent's technology needs.

summary: HP has been awarded Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Americas Company of the Year for its leadership in sustainability and the circular economy in the ICT sector. HP's commitment to sustainable design, circular services, and AI-powered innovation, along with its focus on customer experience, positions it as a leader in responsible innovation and environmental progress.

title: Robbins Geller Rudman & Dowd LLP Announces Proposed Settlement in the HP Securities Litigation

summary: The HP OfficeJet Pro 9015e is a highly rated all-in-one printer for home offices, praised for its speed, sharp print quality, and intuitive HP Smart app integration. It offers features like automatic two-sided scanning, mobile printing, and the HP+ service with Instant Ink delivery and extended warranty. Despite being a bit loud during operation and lacking an SD card slot, its overall performance and features make it a strong contender in its price range.

title: HP CSO Ellen Jackowski on gender, diversity and sustainability
summary: In an interview, HP CSO Ellen Jackowski discusses her career path and HP's commitment to gender equality in leadership by 2030, making it the first Fortune 100 tech company to do so. She also addresses the increasing number of women in CSO roles, attributing it to the urgent need to combat climate change, and emphasizes the importance of diversity and inclusion in the sustainability field.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ) call put ratio 1 calls to 1.3 puts into quarter results
summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) is showing a call-put ratio of 1 call to 1.3 puts ahead of its quarterly results, which are expected after the bell on February 24th. The February 27th weekly call option implied volatility for HPQ is at 124, while the March volatility is at 64, compared to its 52-week range of 25 to 72. The stock is currently trading at $18.30, down 1.56%, with an overall analyst rating of "SELL" and a dividend yield of 6.6%.

title: HP’s Latex Ink is Enjoying Growth in the Inkjet Market
summary: HP's Latex ink technology, a water-based system, has been evolving for nearly two decades, offering key advantages in durability, water resistance, and light fastness, particularly for signage and decoration markets. The company has made significant advancements through four generations of the ink, including improved uniformity, anti-scratch resistance, and the introduction of white Latex ink and broader substrate compatibility. Latex ink's thin, flexible film allows for diverse applications like thermoforming and wallcoverings, supporting its growing adoption in the inkjet market.

title: HP warns US trade regulations, memory chip costs to weigh on annual forecasts
summary: HP Inc. anticipates its fiscal year 2026 results to be at the low end of its projections, attributing this to U.S. trade regulations and rising memory chip costs. Despite these challenges, the company surpassed first-quarter revenue and profit estimates, driven by AI-powered PCs and Windows 11 upgrades. HP's CFO noted the dynamic environment and increasing memory costs as key factors influencing the cautious outlook.

title: HP Inc. (NYSE:HPQ) Stock Falls Despite Earnings Beat on Cautious Outlook
summary: HP Inc. reported strong fiscal first-quarter results for 2026, surpassing analyst expectations for both revenue and earnings per share. However, the stock experienced a significant drop in after-hours trading due to the company's cautious forward guidance, citing potential impacts from U.S. trade regulations and increasing memory chip costs. This discrepancy highlights investor concern over future profitability despite current strong performance.

title: HP Aligns Enterprise Growth with Cost Discipline in Q1
summary: HP Inc. reported strong Q1 fiscal 2026 results with a 7% revenue increase and 9% non-GAAP EPS growth, driven by an 11% rise in Personal Systems revenue due to Windows 11 refresh and AI PC adoption. The company emphasized its "future of work" strategy, integrating AI into its products and services, and announced a partnership with OpenAI for enterprise AI agent deployment. HP projects $1 billion in run-rate savings by FY2028 through internal AI automation, highlighting its focus on leveraging AI for both growth and cost discipline.

title: HP Is Battling Rising Memory Prices
summary: HP is facing challenges due to rising memory prices, which are expected to impact its margins in 2026. Despite cost pressures, Morningstar analysts suggest HPQ shares are becoming cheap. The company operates in consolidated PC and printer markets, with the latter experiencing a slow secular decline.

title: Primecap Management Co. CA Sells 511,675 Shares of HP Inc. $HPQ
summary: Primecap Management Co. CA reduced its stake in HP Inc. by 2.7% in the third quarter, selling 511,675 shares and bringing its total holdings to 18,132,345 shares worth $493,744,000. Other institutional investors have adjusted their positions as well, while HP faces mixed sentiment with Q1 earnings beating estimates but concerns rising over memory chip costs and cautious guidance. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share.

title: HP INC ($HPQ) Former President and CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
summary: HP INC's former President and CEO, Enrique J. Lores, is estimated to have received $23,103,812 in compensation in 2025, which is a 16.2% increase from 2024, according to a recent DEF14A filing. The article also details insider stock transactions, showing multiple sales by executives, and institutional investor activity, with significant changes in holdings by various firms. Additionally, it provides recent analyst ratings and price targets for HPQ stock from several financial institutions.

title: HP Inc. (HPQ): Strong Q1 2026 Revenue Growth Driven by Personal Systems and AI PC Adoption
summary: HP Inc. reported a 7% year-over-year revenue increase in FQ1 2026, reaching $14.4 billion, primarily due to an 11% jump in its Personal Systems segment driven by Windows 11 refresh and AI PC adoption. Despite this growth, the Print segment declined by 2% and rising input costs for DRAM and NAND memory are impacting Personal Systems operating margins, which are expected to remain below long-term targets. HP maintained its full-year guidance but anticipates landing at the lower end of its non-GAAP EPS and free cash flow targets, with a projected double-digit decline in the total addressable market for PC units in CY2026.

title: HP Inc. earnings point to memory inflation challenge
summary: HP Inc.'s recent earnings report indicates that surging DRAM and NAND memory costs, exacerbated by US trade regulations, are significantly impacting profit margins despite a rise in PC revenue and unit shipments. Memory now constitutes about 35% of a PC's bill of materials, doubling its previous weight and pressuring operating income. This shift creates both challenges for ITAD providers and opportunities for those controlling secondary component supply, potentially catalyzing a strategic move towards component-level recovery and remarketing.

title: BlackLine Honors 2025 Partner Award Winners
summary: BlackLine, Inc. has announced the winners of its 2025 Global and Regional Partner Awards, recognizing firms for exceptional dedication to customer success, innovation, and transformative growth in modernizing finance functions. The awards highlight partners like Deloitte and Capgemini for their significant contributions to BlackLine's strategic growth and their role in enabling AI-enabled finance transformation for clients. This annual program underscores the vital role of partners in delivering value and setting new standards in finance transformation.

title: UPDATE 1-Dell shares at three-month highs on forecast it will double AI server revenue
summary: Dell Technologies' shares surged to a three-month high after the company projected its AI server revenue would double in fiscal 2027 to about $50 billion. This forecast, along with a 20% dividend hike and a $10 billion share repurchase program, led to multiple brokerage price target increases. Despite rising memory chip costs impacting the PC market, Dell's leadership in AI compute has allowed it to manage operating margins effectively.
